    content: "# frozen_string_literal: true\n\nrequire \"spec_helper\"\nrequire \"tempfile\"\nrequire
      \"fileutils\"\nrequire_relative \"../../../../lib/coding_agent_tools/molecules/context/context_preset_manager\"\n\nRSpec.describe
      CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Context::ContextPresetManager do\n  let(:temp_dir)
      { Dir.mktmpdir }\n  let(:config_dir) { File.join(temp_dir, \".coding-agent\")
      }\n  let(:config_path) { File.join(config_dir, \"context.yml\") }\n  let(:template_dir)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"docs\", \"context\") }\n  let(:template_file) { File.join(template_dir,
      \"project.md\") }\n  let(:manager) { described_class.new(nil, temp_dir) }\n\n
      \ before do\n    FileUtils.mkdir_p(config_dir)\n    FileUtils.mkdir_p(template_dir)\n
      \   \n    # Create a basic template file\n    File.write(template_file, \"#
      Project Context\\n\\nSample content\")\n  end\n\n  after do\n    FileUtils.rm_rf(temp_dir)
      if Dir.exist?(temp_dir)\n  end\n\n  describe \"#list_presets\" do\n    context
      \"with default configuration\" do\n      it \"returns default presets\" do\n
      \       presets = manager.list_presets\n        \n        expect(presets).to
      be_an(Array)\n        expect(presets.length).to eq(1)\n        \n        project_preset
      = presets.find { |p| p[:name] == \"project\" }\n        expect(project_preset).not_to
      be_nil\n        expect(project_preset[:description]).to eq(\"Main project context\")\n
      \       expect(project_preset[:template]).to include(\"docs/context/project.md\")\n
      \       expect(project_preset[:chunk_limit]).to eq(150_000)\n      end\n    end\n\n
      \   context \"with custom configuration\" do\n      before do\n        File.write(config_path,
      <<~YAML)\n          presets:\n            custom:\n              description:
      \"Custom preset\"\n              template: \"custom/template.md\"\n              output:
      \"custom/output.md\"\n              chunk_limit: 200000\n            project:\n
      \             output: \"custom/project.md\"\n        YAML\n      end\n\n      it
      \"returns both default and custom presets\" do\n        presets = manager.list_presets\n
      \       \n        expect(presets.length).to eq(2)\n        \n        custom_preset
      = presets.find { |p| p[:name] == \"custom\" }\n        expect(custom_preset[:description]).to
      eq(\"Custom preset\")\n        expect(custom_preset[:chunk_limit]).to eq(200_000)\n
      \       \n        project_preset = presets.find { |p| p[:name] == \"project\"
      }\n        expect(project_preset[:output]).to include(\"custom/project.md\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#resolve_preset\" do\n    context
      \"with existing preset\" do\n      it \"resolves preset configuration successfully\"
      do\n        resolved = manager.resolve_preset(\"project\")\n        \n        expect(resolved).to
      be_a(Hash)\n        expect(resolved[:name]).to eq(\"project\")\n        expect(resolved[:description]).to
      eq(\"Main project context\")\n        expect(File.exist?(resolved[:template])).to
      be true\n        expect(resolved[:chunk_limit]).to eq(150_000)\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"with non-existent preset\" do\n      it \"returns nil\"
      do\n        resolved = manager.resolve_preset(\"nonexistent\")\n        expect(resolved).to
      be_nil\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with missing template file\" do\n
      \     before do\n        File.write(config_path, <<~YAML)\n          presets:\n
      \           missing_template:\n              description: \"Missing template\"\n
      \             template: \"missing/template.md\"\n        YAML\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"raises error for missing template\" do\n        expect { manager.resolve_preset(\"missing_template\")
      }.to raise_error(\n          CodingAgentTools::Error, /Template file not found/\n
      \       )\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with forbidden template path\"
      do\n      let(:forbidden_template) { File.join(temp_dir, \".git\", \"template.md\")
      }\n      \n      before do\n        FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(forbidden_template))\n
      \       File.write(forbidden_template, \"content\")\n        \n        File.write(config_path,
      <<~YAML)\n          presets:\n            forbidden:\n              description:
      \"Forbidden template\"\n              template: \".git/template.md\"\n        YAML\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"raises error for forbidden path\" do\n        expect
      { manager.resolve_preset(\"forbidden\") }.to raise_error(\n          CodingAgentTools::Error,
      /not allowed.*forbidden pattern/\n        )\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe
      \"#preset_exists?\" do\n    it \"returns true for existing preset\" do\n      expect(manager.preset_exists?(\"project\")).to
      be true\n    end\n\n    it \"returns false for non-existent preset\" do\n      expect(manager.preset_exists?(\"nonexistent\")).to
      be false\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#default_output_path\" do\n    it \"generates
      default output path in cache directory\" do\n      path = manager.default_output_path(\"project\")\n
      \     expect(path).to include(\"docs/context/cached/project.md\")\n      expect(path).to
      start_with(temp_dir)\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#validate_all_presets\"
      do\n    context \"with valid presets\" do\n      it \"returns validation results
      for all presets\" do\n        results = manager.validate_all_presets\n        \n
      \       expect(results).to be_an(Array)\n        expect(results.length).to eq(1)\n
      \       \n        project_result = results.find { |r| r[:name] == \"project\"
      }\n        expect(project_result[:valid]).to be true\n        expect(project_result[:template_exists]).to
      be true\n        expect(project_result[:message]).to eq(\"Valid\")\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"with invalid presets\" do\n      before do\n        File.write(config_path,
      <<~YAML)\n          presets:\n            invalid:\n              description:
      \"Invalid preset\"\n              template: \"missing/template.md\"\n        YAML\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"returns validation errors\" do\n        results = manager.validate_all_presets\n
      \       \n        invalid_result = results.find { |r| r[:name] == \"invalid\"
      }\n        expect(invalid_result[:valid]).to be false\n        expect(invalid_result[:template_exists]).to
      be false\n        expect(invalid_result[:message]).to include(\"Template file
      not found\")\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"path resolution\" do\n
      \   context \"with relative paths\" do\n      before do\n        File.write(config_path,
      <<~YAML)\n          presets:\n            relative:\n              template:
      \"docs/context/project.md\"\n              output: \"docs/context/cached/relative.md\"\n
      \       YAML\n      end\n\n      it \"resolves relative paths correctly\" do\n
      \       resolved = manager.resolve_preset(\"relative\")\n        \n        expect(resolved[:template]).to
      start_with(temp_dir)\n        expect(resolved[:output]).to start_with(temp_dir)\n
      \       expect(resolved[:template]).to end_with(\"docs/context/project.md\")\n
      \       expect(resolved[:output]).to end_with(\"docs/context/cached/relative.md\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with absolute paths\" do\n      let(:absolute_template)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"docs\", \"absolute_template.md\") }\n      let(:absolute_output)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"docs\", \"absolute_output.md\") }\n      \n      before
      do\n        FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(absolute_template))\n        File.write(absolute_template,
      \"content\")\n        \n        File.write(config_path, <<~YAML)\n          presets:\n
      \           absolute:\n              template: \"#{absolute_template}\"\n              output:
      \"#{absolute_output}\"\n        YAML\n      end\n\n      it \"uses absolute
      paths as-is\" do\n        resolved = manager.resolve_preset(\"absolute\")\n
      \       \n        expect(resolved[:template]).to eq(absolute_template)\n        expect(resolved[:output]).to
      eq(absolute_output)\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"security validation\"
      do\n    let(:allowed_template) { File.join(temp_dir, \"docs\", \"allowed.md\")
      }\n    let(:forbidden_template) { File.join(temp_dir, \".env\") }\n    \n    before
      do\n      File.write(allowed_template, \"content\")\n      File.write(forbidden_template,
      \"secret\")\n    end\n\n    context \"with allowed paths\" do\n      before
      do\n        File.write(config_path, <<~YAML)\n          presets:\n            allowed:\n
      \             template: \"docs/allowed.md\"\n              output: \"docs/output.md\"\n
      \       YAML\n      end\n\n      it \"allows access to permitted paths\" do\n
      \       expect { manager.resolve_preset(\"allowed\") }.not_to raise_error\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with forbidden paths\" do\n      before
      do\n        File.write(config_path, <<~YAML)\n          presets:\n            forbidden_template:\n
      \             template: \".env\"\n            forbidden_output:\n              template:
      \"docs/allowed.md\"\n              output: \".env.output\"\n        YAML\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"blocks access to forbidden template paths\" do\n        expect {
      manager.resolve_preset(\"forbidden_template\") }.to raise_error(\n          CodingAgentTools::Error,
      /not allowed.*forbidden pattern/\n        )\n      end\n\n      it \"blocks
      access to forbidden output paths\" do\n        expect { manager.resolve_preset(\"forbidden_output\")
      }.to raise_error(\n          CodingAgentTools::Error, /not allowed.*forbidden
      pattern/\n        )\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#get_config\"
      do\n    it \"returns loaded configuration\" do\n      config = manager.get_config\n
      \     \n      expect(config).to be_a(Hash)\n      expect(config).to have_key(\"presets\")\n
      \     expect(config).to have_key(\"settings\")\n      expect(config).to have_key(\"security\")\n
      \   end\n  end\nend"
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/molecules/context/document_embedder_spec.rb
    size: 10343
    content: "# frozen_string_literal: true\n\nrequire \"spec_helper\"\nrequire_relative
      \"../../../../lib/coding_agent_tools/molecules/context/document_embedder\"\n\nRSpec.describe
      CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Context::DocumentEmbedder do\n  let(:embedder)
      { described_class.new }\n\n  describe \"#embed_content\" do\n    let(:source_document)
      do\n      <<~MARKDOWN\n        # Project Context\n\n        Some documentation
      content.\n\n        <context-tool-config>\n        files:\n          - docs/*.md\n
      \       format: xml\n        </context-tool-config>\n\n        More content
      here.\n      MARKDOWN\n    end\n\n    let(:processed_content) do\n      <<~CONTENT\n
      \       # Processed Context Results\n\n        Files loaded:\n        - docs/README.md
      (1.2KB)\n        - docs/GUIDE.md (2.3KB)\n\n        Total: 2 files, 3.5KB\n
      \     CONTENT\n    end\n\n    context \"with nil inputs\" do\n      it \"returns
      error for nil source document\" do\n        result = embedder.embed_content(nil,
      processed_content)\n        expect(result[:success]).to be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to
      eq(\"Source document cannot be nil\")\n      end\n\n      it \"returns error
      for nil processed content\" do\n        result = embedder.embed_content(source_document,
      nil)\n        expect(result[:success]).to be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to
      eq(\"Processed content cannot be nil\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"without
      embedding requested\" do\n      let(:options) { {embed_document_source: false}
      }\n\n      it \"returns processed content only\" do\n        result = embedder.embed_content(source_document,
      processed_content, options)\n        \n        expect(result[:success]).to be
      true\n        expect(result[:embedded]).to be false\n        expect(result[:source]).to
      eq(:processed_only)\n        expect(result[:content]).to eq(processed_content)\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with embedding at end\" do\n      let(:options)
      { {embed_document_source: true, embedding_position: :end} }\n\n      it \"embeds
      content at end of document\" do\n        result = embedder.embed_content(source_document,
      processed_content, options)\n        \n        expect(result[:success]).to be
      true\n        expect(result[:embedded]).to be true\n        expect(result[:strategy]).to
      eq(:end)\n        expect(result[:source]).to eq(:full_document_with_embedded)\n
      \       \n        # Check that original content is preserved\n        expect(result[:content]).to
      include(\"# Project Context\")\n        expect(result[:content]).to include(\"<context-tool-config>\")\n
      \       \n        # Check that processed content is added at end\n        expect(result[:content]).to
      end_with(processed_content.strip)\n        expect(result[:content]).to include(\"<!--
      PROCESSED CONTEXT -->\")\n      end\n\n      it \"uses custom marker when provided\"
      do\n        custom_options = options.merge(embedding_marker: \"<!-- CUSTOM MARKER
      -->\")\n        result = embedder.embed_content(source_document, processed_content,
      custom_options)\n        \n        expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:content]).to
      include(\"<!-- CUSTOM MARKER -->\")\n        expect(result[:marker]).to eq(\"<!--
      CUSTOM MARKER -->\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with embedding after
      config block\" do\n      let(:options) { {embed_document_source: true, embedding_position:
      :after_config} }\n\n      it \"embeds content after config block\" do\n        result
      = embedder.embed_content(source_document, processed_content, options)\n        \n
      \       expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:embedded]).to
      be true\n        expect(result[:strategy]).to eq(:after_config)\n        \n
      \       # Check that content is inserted after </context-tool-config>\n        config_end_index
      = result[:content].index(\"</context-tool-config>\")\n        marker_index =
      result[:content].index(\"<!-- PROCESSED CONTEXT -->\")\n        \n        expect(marker_index).to
      be > config_end_index\n        expect(result[:content]).to include(processed_content.strip)\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"falls back to end embedding when no config block found\"
      do\n        simple_document = \"# Simple Document\\n\\nNo config blocks here.\"\n
      \       result = embedder.embed_content(simple_document, processed_content,
      options)\n        \n        expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:strategy]).to
      eq(:end)\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with config block replacement\"
      do\n      let(:options) { {embed_document_source: true, embedding_position:
      :replace_config} }\n\n      it \"replaces config blocks with processed content\"
      do\n        result = embedder.embed_content(source_document, processed_content,
      options)\n        \n        expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:embedded]).to
      be true\n        expect(result[:strategy]).to eq(:replace_config)\n        expect(result[:source]).to
      eq(:replaced_config_blocks)\n        \n        # Check that config block is
      replaced\n        expect(result[:content]).not_to include(\"<context-tool-config>\")\n
      \       expect(result[:content]).not_to include(\"</context-tool-config>\")\n
      \       expect(result[:content]).to include(processed_content.strip)\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"falls back to end embedding when no config blocks found\" do\n        simple_document
      = \"# Simple Document\\n\\nNo config blocks here.\"\n        result = embedder.embed_content(simple_document,
      processed_content, options)\n        \n        expect(result[:success]).to be
      true\n        expect(result[:strategy]).to eq(:end)\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n
      \ describe \"#should_embed?\" do\n    it \"returns true when embed_document_source
      is true\" do\n      options = {embed_document_source: true}\n      expect(embedder.should_embed?(options)).to
      be true\n    end\n\n    it \"returns false when embed_document_source is false\"
      do\n      options = {embed_document_source: false}\n      expect(embedder.should_embed?(options)).to
      be false\n    end\n\n    it \"checks YAML config for embedding directive\" do\n
      \     yaml_config = {\"embed_document_source\" => true}\n      options = {yaml_config:
      yaml_config}\n      expect(embedder.should_embed?(options)).to be true\n    end\n\n
      \   it \"returns false by default\" do\n      options = {}\n      expect(embedder.should_embed?(options)).to
      be false\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#remove_existing_embedded_content\"
      do\n    let(:document_with_embedded) do\n      <<~MARKDOWN\n        # Original
      Content\n\n        Some text here.\n\n        <!-- PROCESSED CONTEXT -->\n\n
      \       Previous embedded content\n        that should be removed.\n      MARKDOWN\n
      \   end\n\n    it \"removes existing embedded content\" do\n      marker = \"<!--
      PROCESSED CONTEXT -->\"\n      cleaned = embedder.remove_existing_embedded_content(document_with_embedded,
      marker)\n      \n      expect(cleaned).to include(\"# Original Content\")\n
      \     expect(cleaned).to include(\"Some text here.\")\n      expect(cleaned).not_to
      include(\"Previous embedded content\")\n      expect(cleaned).not_to include(\"<!--
      PROCESSED CONTEXT -->\")\n    end\n\n    it \"handles documents without embedded
      content\" do\n      simple_document = \"# Simple Document\\n\\nNo embedded content.\"\n
      \     marker = \"<!-- PROCESSED CONTEXT -->\"\n      cleaned = embedder.remove_existing_embedded_content(simple_document,
      marker)\n      \n      expect(cleaned).to eq(simple_document.strip)\n    end\n
      \ end\n\n  describe \"#validate_embedding_options\" do\n    it \"validates valid
      options\" do\n      options = {\n        embedding_position: :end,\n        embedding_marker:
      \"<!-- CUSTOM -->\"\n      }\n      \n      result = embedder.validate_embedding_options(options)\n
      \     expect(result[:valid]).to be true\n      expect(result[:errors]).to be_empty\n
      \   end\n\n    it \"rejects invalid embedding position\" do\n      options =
      {embedding_position: :invalid_position}\n      \n      result = embedder.validate_embedding_options(options)\n
      \     expect(result[:valid]).to be false\n      expect(result[:errors]).to include(/Invalid
      embedding position/)\n    end\n\n    it \"rejects empty embedding marker\" do\n
      \     options = {embedding_marker: \"   \"}\n      \n      result = embedder.validate_embedding_options(options)\n
      \     expect(result[:valid]).to be false\n      expect(result[:errors]).to include(/Embedding
      marker cannot be empty/)\n    end\n\n    it \"rejects marker with newlines\"
      do\n      options = {embedding_marker: \"<!-- MARKER\\nWITH NEWLINE -->\"}\n
      \     \n      result = embedder.validate_embedding_options(options)\n      expect(result[:valid]).to
      be false\n      expect(result[:errors]).to include(/Embedding marker cannot
      contain newlines/)\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#embedding_summary\" do\n
      \   it \"provides summary for successful embedding\" do\n      result = {\n
      \       success: true,\n        embedded: true,\n        strategy: :end,\n        source:
      :full_document_with_embedded,\n        marker: \"<!-- TEST -->\",\n        content:
      \"test content\"\n      }\n      \n      summary = embedder.embedding_summary(result)\n
      \     expect(summary).to include(\"Content embedded successfully\")\n      expect(summary).to
      include(\"Strategy: end\")\n      expect(summary).to include(\"Source: full_document_with_embedded\")\n
      \     expect(summary).to include(\"Marker: <!-- TEST -->\")\n    end\n\n    it
      \"provides summary for non-embedded result\" do\n      result = {\n        success:
      true,\n        embedded: false,\n        source: :processed_only,\n        content:
      \"test content\"\n      }\n      \n      summary = embedder.embedding_summary(result)\n
      \     expect(summary).to include(\"Content returned without embedding\")\n      expect(summary).to
      include(\"Source: processed_only\")\n    end\n\n    it \"provides error summary
      for failed embedding\" do\n      result = {\n        success: false,\n        error:
      \"Test error message\"\n      }\n      \n      summary = embedder.embedding_summary(result)\n
      \     expect(summary).to eq(\"Embedding failed: Test error message\")\n    end\n
      \ end\n\n  describe \"#extract_yaml_config\" do\n    it \"parses valid YAML\"
      do\n      yaml_content = \"embed_document_source: true\\nformat: xml\"\n      config
      = embedder.extract_yaml_config(yaml_content)\n      \n      expect(config[\"embed_document_source\"]).to
      be true\n      expect(config[\"format\"]).to eq(\"xml\")\n    end\n\n    it
      \"returns empty hash for invalid YAML\" do\n      yaml_content = \"invalid:
      [unclosed bracket\"\n      config = embedder.extract_yaml_config(yaml_content)\n
      \     \n      expect(config).to eq({})\n    end\n\n    it \"returns empty hash
      for nil or empty input\" do\n      expect(embedder.extract_yaml_config(nil)).to
      eq({})\n      expect(embedder.extract_yaml_config(\"\")).to eq({})\n    end\n
      \ end\nend"
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/molecules/context/input_format_detector_spec.rb
    size: 6881
    content: "# frozen_string_literal: true\n\nrequire \"spec_helper\"\nrequire_relative
      \"../../../../lib/coding_agent_tools/molecules/context/input_format_detector\"\n\nRSpec.describe
      CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Context::InputFormatDetector do\n  let(:detector)
      { described_class.new }\n\n  describe \"#detect_format\" do\n    context \"with
      nil input\" do\n      it \"returns error for nil input\" do\n        result
      = detector.detect_format(nil)\n        expect(result[:success]).to be false\n
      \       expect(result[:error]).to eq(\"Input cannot be nil\")\n      end\n    end\n\n
      \   context \"with empty input\" do\n      it \"returns error for empty input\"
      do\n        result = detector.detect_format(\"\")\n        expect(result[:success]).to
      be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to eq(\"Input cannot be empty\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with YAML file\" do\n      let(:yaml_file)
      { \"template.yml\" }\n      let(:yaml_content) { \"files:\\n  - docs/*.md\\nformat:
      xml\" }\n\n      before do\n        allow(File).to receive(:exist?).with(yaml_file).and_return(true)\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"detects YAML file format\" do\n        result = detector.detect_format(yaml_file)\n
      \       expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:format]).to
      eq(:yaml_file)\n        expect(result[:file_path]).to eq(yaml_file)\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"with agent markdown file\" do\n      let(:agent_file)
      { \"task-finder.ag.md\" }\n\n      before do\n        allow(File).to receive(:exist?).with(agent_file).and_return(true)\n
      \       # Mock file reading for old agent format (no context-tool-config tags)\n
      \       allow(File).to receive(:read).with(agent_file).and_return(\"# Agent:
      Task Finder\\n\\n## Context Definition\\nfiles:\\n  - '**/*.md'\")\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"detects agent file format\" do\n        result = detector.detect_format(agent_file)\n
      \       expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:format]).to
      eq(:agent_file)\n        expect(result[:file_path]).to eq(agent_file)\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"with regular markdown file\" do\n      let(:md_file)
      { \"context.md\" }\n\n      before do\n        allow(File).to receive(:exist?).with(md_file).and_return(true)\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"detects markdown file format\" do\n        result =
      detector.detect_format(md_file)\n        expect(result[:success]).to be true\n
      \       expect(result[:format]).to eq(:markdown_file)\n        expect(result[:file_path]).to
      eq(md_file)\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with YAML string content\"
      do\n      let(:yaml_string) { \"files:\\n  - docs/*.md\\ncommands:\\n  - git
      status\" }\n\n      it \"detects YAML string format\" do\n        result = detector.detect_format(yaml_string)\n
      \       expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:format]).to
      eq(:yaml_string)\n        expect(result[:content]).to eq(yaml_string)\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"with markdown string containing context tags\" do\n
      \     let(:markdown_string) do\n        <<~MARKDOWN\n          # Project Context\n
      \         \n          <context-tool-config>\n          files:\n            -
      docs/*.md\n          </context-tool-config>\n        MARKDOWN\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"detects markdown string format\" do\n        result = detector.detect_format(markdown_string)\n
      \       expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:format]).to
      eq(:markdown_string)\n        expect(result[:content]).to eq(markdown_string)\n
      \     end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#looks_like_file_path?\" do\n    it
      \"returns true for paths with separators\" do\n      expect(detector.looks_like_file_path?(\"docs/file.md\")).to
      be true\n      expect(detector.looks_like_file_path?(\"../file.yml\")).to be
      true\n      expect(detector.looks_like_file_path?(\"file.txt\")).to be true\n
      \   end\n\n    it \"returns false for plain strings\" do\n      expect(detector.looks_like_file_path?(\"files:
      docs\")).to be false\n      expect(detector.looks_like_file_path?(\"plain text\")).to
      be false\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#detect_file_format\" do\n    it \"detects
      YAML files\" do\n      expect(detector.detect_file_format(\"template.yml\")).to
      eq(:yaml_file)\n      expect(detector.detect_file_format(\"config.yaml\")).to
      eq(:yaml_file)\n    end\n\n    it \"detects agent markdown files\" do\n      expect(detector.detect_file_format(\"task-finder.ag.md\")).to
      eq(:agent_file)\n      expect(detector.detect_file_format(\"search.ag.md\")).to
      eq(:agent_file)\n    end\n\n    it \"detects regular markdown files\" do\n      expect(detector.detect_file_format(\"README.md\")).to
      eq(:markdown_file)\n      expect(detector.detect_file_format(\"context.md\")).to
      eq(:markdown_file)\n    end\n\n    it \"returns unknown for unrecognized extensions\"
      do\n      expect(detector.detect_file_format(\"file.txt\")).to eq(:unknown)\n
      \     expect(detector.detect_file_format(\"script.rb\")).to eq(:unknown)\n    end\n
      \ end\n\n  describe \"#has_context_config_tag?\" do\n    it \"returns true when
      tags are present\" do\n      content = \"<context-tool-config>\\nfiles: []\\n</context-tool-config>\"\n
      \     expect(detector.has_context_config_tag?(content)).to be true\n    end\n\n
      \   it \"returns false when tags are absent\" do\n      content = \"Regular
      markdown content without tags\"\n      expect(detector.has_context_config_tag?(content)).to
      be false\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#looks_like_yaml?\" do\n    it \"returns
      true for YAML content with document separator\" do\n      content = \"---\\nfiles:\\n
      \ - docs/*.md\"\n      expect(detector.looks_like_yaml?(content)).to be true\n
      \   end\n\n    it \"returns true for YAML content with key-value pairs\" do\n
      \     content = \"files:\\n  - docs/*.md\\nformat: xml\"\n      expect(detector.looks_like_yaml?(content)).to
      be true\n    end\n\n    it \"returns false for content with context tags\" do\n
      \     content = \"<context-tool-config>\\nfiles: []\\n</context-tool-config>\"\n
      \     expect(detector.looks_like_yaml?(content)).to be false\n    end\n\n    it
      \"returns false for plain text\" do\n      content = \"This is just plain text
      without structure\"\n      expect(detector.looks_like_yaml?(content)).to be
      false\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#format_description\" do\n    it \"returns
      descriptions for all supported formats\" do\n      expect(detector.format_description(:yaml_file)).to
      eq(\"YAML template file\")\n      expect(detector.format_description(:agent_file)).to
      eq(\"Agent markdown file (.ag.md)\")\n      expect(detector.format_description(:markdown_file)).to
      eq(\"Instruction markdown file with <context-tool-config> tags\")\n      expect(detector.format_description(:unknown)).to
      eq(\"Unknown format\")\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#supported_format?\"
      do\n    it \"returns true for supported formats\" do\n      expect(detector.supported_format?(:yaml_file)).to
      be true\n      expect(detector.supported_format?(:agent_file)).to be true\n
      \     expect(detector.supported_format?(:markdown_file)).to be true\n    end\n\n
      \   it \"returns false for unsupported formats\" do\n      expect(detector.supported_format?(:unknown)).to
      be false\n      expect(detector.supported_format?(:unsupported)).to be false\n
      \   end\n  end\nend"
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/molecules/context/markdown_yaml_extractor_spec.rb
    size: 10895
    content: "# frozen_string_literal: true\n\nrequire \"spec_helper\"\nrequire_relative
      \"../../../../lib/coding_agent_tools/molecules/context/markdown_yaml_extractor\"\n\nRSpec.describe
      CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Context::MarkdownYamlExtractor do\n  let(:extractor)
      { described_class.new }\n\n  describe \"#extract_yaml_from_markdown\" do\n    context
      \"with nil or empty content\" do\n      it \"returns error for nil content\"
      do\n        result = extractor.extract_yaml_from_markdown(nil)\n        expect(result[:success]).to
      be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to eq(\"Content cannot be nil\")\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"returns error for empty content\" do\n        result
      = extractor.extract_yaml_from_markdown(\"\")\n        expect(result[:success]).to
      be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to eq(\"Content cannot be empty\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with tagged blocks format\" do\n      let(:markdown_with_tags)
      do\n        <<~MARKDOWN\n          # Project Context\n\n          Some documentation
      content.\n\n          <context-tool-config>\n          files:\n            -
      docs/*.md\n            - README.md\n          commands:\n            - git-status\n
      \         format: markdown-xml\n          </context-tool-config>\n\n          More
      content here.\n        MARKDOWN\n      end\n\n      it \"extracts YAML from
      tagged blocks\" do\n        result = extractor.extract_yaml_from_markdown(markdown_with_tags)\n
      \       \n        expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:source_format]).to
      eq(:tagged_blocks)\n        expect(result[:template][:files]).to eq([\"docs/*.md\",
      \"README.md\"])\n        expect(result[:template][:commands]).to eq([\"git-status\"])\n
      \       expect(result[:template][:format]).to eq(\"markdown-xml\")\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"includes the extracted YAML content\" do\n        result = extractor.extract_yaml_from_markdown(markdown_with_tags)\n
      \       \n        expect(result[:yaml_content]).to include(\"files:\")\n        expect(result[:yaml_content]).to
      include(\"- docs/*.md\")\n        expect(result[:yaml_content]).to include(\"commands:\")\n
      \       expect(result[:yaml_content]).to include(\"format: markdown-xml\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with multiple tagged blocks\" do\n      let(:markdown_with_multiple_blocks)
      do\n        <<~MARKDOWN\n          # Project Context\n\n          <context-tool-config>\n
      \         files:\n            - docs/*.md\n          format: xml\n          </context-tool-config>\n\n
      \         Some content between blocks.\n\n          <context-tool-config>\n
      \         files:\n            - src/*.rb\n          format: yaml\n          </context-tool-config>\n\n
      \         End content.\n        MARKDOWN\n      end\n\n      it \"uses the first
      block and warns about others\" do\n        result = extractor.extract_yaml_from_markdown(markdown_with_multiple_blocks)\n
      \       \n        expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:source_format]).to
      eq(:tagged_blocks)\n        expect(result[:total_blocks]).to eq(2)\n        expect(result[:warning]).to
      include(\"Multiple <context-tool-config> blocks found\")\n        expect(result[:template][:format]).to
      eq(\"xml\") # From first block\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with legacy
      Context Definition format\" do\n      let(:markdown_with_legacy_format) do\n
      \       <<~MARKDOWN\n          # Agent Documentation\n\n          ## Core Functionality\n
      \         This agent does important work.\n\n          ## Context Definition\n\n
      \         ```yaml\n          files:\n            - legacy/*.md\n          commands:\n
      \           - task-manager list\n          format: markdown-xml\n          ```\n\n
      \         ## Error Handling\n          Handle errors gracefully.\n        MARKDOWN\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"extracts YAML from legacy format\" do\n        result
      = extractor.extract_yaml_from_markdown(markdown_with_legacy_format)\n        \n
      \       expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:source_format]).to
      eq(:context_definition)\n        expect(result[:template][:files]).to eq([\"legacy/*.md\"])\n
      \       expect(result[:template][:commands]).to eq([\"task-manager list\"])\n
      \       expect(result[:template][:format]).to eq(\"markdown-xml\")\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"with invalid YAML in tagged blocks\" do\n      let(:markdown_with_invalid_yaml)
      do\n        <<~MARKDOWN\n          # Project Context\n\n          <context-tool-config>\n
      \         files:\n            - docs/*.md\n          invalid_yaml: [unclosed
      bracket]\n          format: xml\n          \n          \n          invalid_key_structure:
      {\n          </context-tool-config>\n        MARKDOWN\n      end\n\n      it
      \"returns error for invalid YAML\" do\n        result = extractor.extract_yaml_from_markdown(markdown_with_invalid_yaml)\n
      \       \n        expect(result[:success]).to be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to
      match(/parse|template/)\n        expect(result[:source_format]).to eq(:tagged_blocks)\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with no extractable configuration\" do\n
      \     let(:plain_markdown) do\n        <<~MARKDOWN\n          # Regular Markdown\n\n
      \         This is just regular markdown content without any\n          context
      configuration blocks or sections.\n\n          ## Some Section\n          Regular
      content here.\n        MARKDOWN\n      end\n\n      it \"returns error when
      no configuration found\" do\n        result = extractor.extract_yaml_from_markdown(plain_markdown)\n
      \       \n        expect(result[:success]).to be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to
      include(\"No <context-tool-config> blocks or Context Definition sections found\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#find_context_tool_config_blocks\"
      do\n    it \"finds single block\" do\n      content = <<~MARKDOWN\n        <context-tool-config>\n
      \       files: [docs/*.md]\n        </context-tool-config>\n      MARKDOWN\n
      \     \n      blocks = extractor.find_context_tool_config_blocks(content)\n
      \     expect(blocks.length).to eq(1)\n      expect(blocks.first).to include(\"files:
      [docs/*.md]\")\n    end\n\n    it \"finds multiple blocks\" do\n      content
      = <<~MARKDOWN\n        <context-tool-config>\n        files: [docs/*.md]\n        </context-tool-config>\n
      \       \n        <context-tool-config>\n        commands: [git status]\n        </context-tool-config>\n
      \     MARKDOWN\n      \n      blocks = extractor.find_context_tool_config_blocks(content)\n
      \     expect(blocks.length).to eq(2)\n      expect(blocks.first).to include(\"files:
      [docs/*.md]\")\n      expect(blocks.last).to include(\"commands: [git status]\")\n
      \   end\n\n    it \"returns empty array when no blocks found\" do\n      content
      = \"Regular markdown content without tagged blocks\"\n      blocks = extractor.find_context_tool_config_blocks(content)\n
      \     expect(blocks).to be_empty\n    end\n\n    it \"ignores empty blocks\"
      do\n      content = <<~MARKDOWN\n        <context-tool-config>\n        files:
      [docs/*.md]\n        </context-tool-config>\n        \n        <context-tool-config>\n
      \       \n        </context-tool-config>\n      MARKDOWN\n      \n      blocks
      = extractor.find_context_tool_config_blocks(content)\n      expect(blocks.length).to
      eq(1)\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#has_extractable_config?\" do\n    it
      \"returns true for content with tagged blocks\" do\n      content = <<~MARKDOWN\n
      \       <context-tool-config>\n        files: [docs/*.md]\n        </context-tool-config>\n
      \     MARKDOWN\n      \n      expect(extractor.has_extractable_config?(content)).to
      be true\n    end\n\n    it \"returns true for content with legacy Context Definition\"
      do\n      content = <<~MARKDOWN\n        ## Context Definition\n        ```yaml\n
      \       files: [docs/*.md]\n        ```\n      MARKDOWN\n      \n      expect(extractor.has_extractable_config?(content)).to
      be true\n    end\n\n    it \"returns false for content without configuration\"
      do\n      content = \"Regular markdown content\"\n      expect(extractor.has_extractable_config?(content)).to
      be false\n    end\n\n    it \"returns false for nil or empty content\" do\n
      \     expect(extractor.has_extractable_config?(nil)).to be false\n      expect(extractor.has_extractable_config?(\"\")).to
      be false\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#validate_markdown_config\" do\n    context
      \"with valid tagged blocks\" do\n      let(:valid_content) do\n        <<~MARKDOWN\n
      \         <context-tool-config>\n          files: [docs/*.md]\n          format:
      xml\n          </context-tool-config>\n        MARKDOWN\n      end\n\n      it
      \"returns valid result\" do\n        result = extractor.validate_markdown_config(valid_content)\n
      \       \n        expect(result[:valid]).to be true\n        expect(result[:source_format]).to
      eq(:tagged_blocks)\n        expect(result[:template]).to be_a(Hash)\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"with valid legacy format\" do\n      let(:legacy_content)
      do\n        <<~MARKDOWN\n          ## Context Definition\n          ```yaml\n
      \         files: [legacy/*.md]\n          ```\n        MARKDOWN\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"returns valid result\" do\n        result = extractor.validate_markdown_config(legacy_content)\n
      \       \n        expect(result[:valid]).to be true\n        expect(result[:source_format]).to
      eq(:context_definition)\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with invalid content\"
      do\n      it \"returns invalid for content without configuration\" do\n        result
      = extractor.validate_markdown_config(\"Regular content\")\n        \n        expect(result[:valid]).to
      be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to include(\"No <context-tool-config>
      blocks or Context Definition sections found\")\n      end\n\n      it \"returns
      invalid for nil content\" do\n        result = extractor.validate_markdown_config(nil)\n
      \       \n        expect(result[:valid]).to be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to
      eq(\"Content cannot be nil\")\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#extraction_summary\"
      do\n    it \"provides summary for successful tagged extraction\" do\n      result
      = {\n        success: true,\n        source_format: :tagged_blocks,\n        total_blocks:
      1,\n        template: {\n          files: [\"docs/*.md\"],\n          commands:
      [\"git-status\"],\n          format: \"xml\"\n        }\n      }\n      \n      summary
      = extractor.extraction_summary(result)\n      expect(summary).to include(\"YAML
      extracted successfully\")\n      expect(summary).to include(\"Source: <context-tool-config>
      tagged blocks\")\n      expect(summary).to include(\"Files: 1 pattern(s)\")\n
      \     expect(summary).to include(\"Commands: 1 command(s)\")\n      expect(summary).to
      include(\"Format: xml\")\n    end\n\n    it \"provides summary for legacy extraction\"
      do\n      result = {\n        success: true,\n        source_format: :context_definition,\n
      \       template: {\n          files: [\"legacy/*.md\"],\n          commands:
      [],\n          format: \"yaml\"\n        }\n      }\n      \n      summary =
      extractor.extraction_summary(result)\n      expect(summary).to include(\"Source:
      Legacy Context Definition section\")\n    end\n\n    it \"provides error summary
      for failed extraction\" do\n      result = {\n        success: false,\n        error:
      \"Invalid YAML format\"\n      }\n      \n      summary = extractor.extraction_summary(result)\n
      \     expect(summary).to eq(\"Extraction failed: Invalid YAML format\")\n    end\n
      \ end\nend"
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/molecules/context_loader_spec.rb
    size: 15775
    content: "# frozen_string_literal: true\n\nrequire \"spec_helper\"\nrequire \"tmpdir\"\nrequire
      \"fileutils\"\n\nRSpec.describe CodingAgentTools::Molecules::ContextLoader do\n
      \ let(:temp_dir) { Dir.mktmpdir }\n  let(:project_root) { temp_dir }\n  let(:docs_dir)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"docs\") }\n  let(:mock_sandbox) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::ProjectSandbox)
      }\n\n  before do\n    # Set up basic project structure\n    FileUtils.mkdir_p(docs_dir)\n\n
      \   # Mock sandbox to return our temp directory as project root\n    allow(mock_sandbox).to
      receive(:project_root).and_return(project_root)\n  end\n\n  after do\n    safe_directory_cleanup(temp_dir)\n
      \ end\n\n  describe \"#initialize\" do\n    it \"accepts a custom sandbox\"
      do\n      context_loader = described_class.new(mock_sandbox)\n      expect(context_loader.instance_variable_get(:@sandbox)).to
      eq(mock_sandbox)\n    end\n\n    it \"creates a default ProjectSandbox when
      none provided\" do\n      context_loader = described_class.new\n      sandbox
      = context_loader.instance_variable_get(:@sandbox)\n      expect(sandbox).to
      be_a(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::ProjectSandbox)\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe
      \"#load_docs_context\" do\n    let(:context_loader) { described_class.new(mock_sandbox)
      }\n\n    context \"when docs directory does not exist\" do\n      before do\n
      \       FileUtils.rmdir(docs_dir)\n      end\n\n      it \"returns failure result
      with error message\" do\n        result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n\n
      \       expect(result[:success]).to be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to
      include(\"Docs directory not found\")\n        expect(result[:error]).to include(docs_dir)\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"when docs directory exists but is empty\"
      do\n      it \"returns success with empty context\" do\n        result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n\n
      \       expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:context]).to
      eq(\"\")\n        expect(result[:files_loaded]).to eq(0)\n        expect(result[:files_failed]).to
      eq(0)\n        expect(result[:failed_files]).to be_nil\n      end\n    end\n\n
      \   context \"when docs directory contains markdown files\" do\n      before
      do\n        # Create test markdown files\n        File.write(File.join(docs_dir,
      \"README.md\"), \"# Main Documentation\\n\\nThis is the main docs.\")\n        File.write(File.join(docs_dir,
      \"guide.md\"), \"# User Guide\\n\\nStep-by-step instructions.\")\n\n        #
      Create subdirectory with markdown file\n        subdir = File.join(docs_dir,
      \"api\")\n        FileUtils.mkdir_p(subdir)\n        File.write(File.join(subdir,
      \"endpoints.md\"), \"# API Endpoints\\n\\nREST API documentation.\")\n\n        #
      Create non-markdown file (should be ignored)\n        File.write(File.join(docs_dir,
      \"config.txt\"), \"Some config content\")\n      end\n\n      it \"loads all
      markdown files successfully\" do\n        result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n\n
      \       expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:files_loaded]).to
      eq(3)\n        expect(result[:files_failed]).to eq(0)\n        expect(result[:failed_files]).to
      be_nil\n      end\n\n      it \"generates proper XML embedded context format\"
      do\n        result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n        context = result[:context]\n\n
      \       expect(context).to start_with(\"<context>\\n\")\n        expect(context).to
      end_with(\"</context>\")\n\n        # Should contain all three markdown files\n
      \       expect(context).to include('path=\"docs/README.md\"')\n        expect(context).to
      include('path=\"docs/guide.md\"')\n        expect(context).to include('path=\"docs/api/endpoints.md\"')\n\n
      \       # Should contain file contents\n        expect(context).to include(\"#
      Main Documentation\")\n        expect(context).to include(\"# User Guide\")\n
      \       expect(context).to include(\"# API Endpoints\")\n\n        # Should
      not contain non-markdown files\n        expect(context).not_to include(\"config.txt\")\n
      \       expect(context).not_to include(\"Some config content\")\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"indents content properly for XML structure\" do\n        result =
      context_loader.load_docs_context\n        context = result[:context]\n\n        #
      Check that content is properly indented (8 spaces)\n        lines = context.split(\"\\n\")\n
      \       content_lines = lines.select { |line| line.start_with?(\"        \")
      && !line.strip.empty? }\n        expect(content_lines).not_to be_empty\n\n        #
      Verify document structure\n        expect(context).to match(/    <document path=\"[^\"]+\">/)\n
      \       expect(context).to match(/        # .+/)\n        expect(context).to
      match(/    <\\/document>/)\n      end\n\n      it \"sorts files alphabetically\"
      do\n        result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n        context = result[:context]\n\n
      \       # Extract the order of file paths from the context\n        path_matches
      = context.scan(/path=\"([^\"]+)\"/)\n        paths = path_matches.flatten\n\n
      \       expect(paths).to eq(paths.sort)\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when
      some files are unreadable\" do\n      before do\n        File.write(File.join(docs_dir,
      \"readable.md\"), \"# Readable File\\n\\nContent here.\")\n\n        # Create
      an unreadable file by making it write-only\n        unreadable_file = File.join(docs_dir,
      \"unreadable.md\")\n        File.write(unreadable_file, \"# Unreadable File\")\n
      \       File.chmod(0o000, unreadable_file)\n      end\n\n      after do\n        #
      Restore permissions for cleanup\n        unreadable_file = File.join(docs_dir,
      \"unreadable.md\")\n        File.chmod(0o644, unreadable_file) if File.exist?(unreadable_file)\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"loads readable files and reports failed files\" do\n
      \       result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n\n        expect(result[:success]).to
      be true\n        expect(result[:files_loaded]).to eq(1)\n        expect(result[:files_failed]).to
      eq(1)\n        expect(result[:failed_files]).to be_an(Array)\n        expect(result[:failed_files].length).to
      eq(1)\n\n        failed_file = result[:failed_files].first\n        expect(failed_file[:path]).to
      eq(\"docs/unreadable.md\")\n        expect(failed_file[:error]).to be_a(String)\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"includes readable file content in context\" do\n        result
      = context_loader.load_docs_context\n        context = result[:context]\n\n        expect(context).to
      include(\"# Readable File\")\n        expect(context).to include('path=\"docs/readable.md\"')\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"when an exception occurs during processing\"
      do\n      before do\n        # Create a file to ensure docs directory exists\n
      \       File.write(File.join(docs_dir, \"test.md\"), \"# Test\")\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"returns failure result with error message\" do\n        # Mock Dir.glob
      to raise an exception\n        allow(Dir).to receive(:glob).and_raise(StandardError.new(\"Filesystem
      error\"))\n\n        result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n\n        expect(result[:success]).to
      be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to include(\"Context loading failed\")\n
      \       expect(result[:error]).to include(\"Filesystem error\")\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"with complex file content\" do\n      before do\n        #
      Test file with various content types\n        complex_content = <<~MARKDOWN\n
      \         # Complex Document\n          \n          This document contains various
      elements:\n          \n          ## Code Block\n          ```ruby\n          def
      hello_world\n            puts \"Hello, World!\"\n          end\n          ```\n
      \         \n          ## Special Characters\n          - Quotes: \"double\"
      and 'single'\n          - Ampersands: A & B\n          - Less than: <script>\n
      \         - Greater than: >\n          \n          ## Unicode\n          Emoji:
      \U0001F680 \U0001F4DD ✅\n          Accents: café, naïve, résumé\n        MARKDOWN\n\n
      \       File.write(File.join(docs_dir, \"complex.md\"), complex_content)\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"preserves all content including special characters\" do\n        result
      = context_loader.load_docs_context\n        context = result[:context]\n\n        expect(context).to
      include(\"def hello_world\")\n        expect(context).to include('puts \"Hello,
      World!\"')\n        expect(context).to include(\"\\\"double\\\" and 'single'\")\n
      \       expect(context).to include(\"A & B\")\n        expect(context).to include(\"<script>\")\n
      \       expect(context).to include(\"\U0001F680 \U0001F4DD ✅\")\n        expect(context).to
      include(\"café, naïve, résumé\")\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#format_embedded_context\"
      do\n    let(:context_loader) { described_class.new(mock_sandbox) }\n\n    context
      \"with empty documents array\" do\n      it \"returns empty context tags\" do\n
      \       result = context_loader.send(:format_embedded_context, [])\n        expect(result).to
      eq(\"\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with single document\" do\n      let(:documents)
      do\n        [{\n          path: \"docs/single.md\",\n          content: \"#
      Single Document\\n\\nSimple content.\"\n        }]\n      end\n\n      it \"formats
      single document correctly\" do\n        result = context_loader.send(:format_embedded_context,
      documents)\n\n        expect(result).to start_with(\"<context>\\n\")\n        expect(result).to
      end_with(\"</context>\")\n        expect(result).to include('    <document path=\"docs/single.md\">')\n
      \       expect(result).to include(\"        # Single Document\")\n        expect(result).to
      include(\"        Simple content.\")\n        expect(result).to include(\"    </document>\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with multiple documents\" do\n      let(:documents)
      do\n        [\n          {\n            path: \"docs/first.md\",\n            content:
      \"# First\\nContent 1\"\n          },\n          {\n            path: \"docs/second.md\",\n
      \           content: \"# Second\\nContent 2\"\n          }\n        ]\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"formats multiple documents correctly\" do\n        result = context_loader.send(:format_embedded_context,
      documents)\n\n        expect(result).to include('    <document path=\"docs/first.md\">')\n
      \       expect(result).to include(\"        # First\")\n        expect(result).to
      include(\"        Content 1\")\n        expect(result).to include('    <document
      path=\"docs/second.md\">')\n        expect(result).to include(\"        # Second\")\n
      \       expect(result).to include(\"        Content 2\")\n\n        # Verify
      proper document separation\n        first_doc_end = result.index(\"    </document>\")\n
      \       second_doc_start = result.index('    <document path=\"docs/second.md\">')\n
      \       expect(second_doc_start).to be > first_doc_end\n      end\n    end\n\n
      \   context \"with document containing no newlines\" do\n      let(:documents)
      do\n        [{\n          path: \"docs/oneline.md\",\n          content: \"Single
      line content without newline\"\n        }]\n      end\n\n      it \"handles
      content without trailing newlines\" do\n        result = context_loader.send(:format_embedded_context,
      documents)\n\n        expect(result).to include(\"        Single line content
      without newline\")\n        expect(result).to include(\"    </document>\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with document containing multiple newlines\"
      do\n      let(:documents) do\n        [{\n          path: \"docs/multiline.md\",\n
      \         content: \"Line 1\\n\\nLine 3\\n\\n\\nLine 6\\n\"\n        }]\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"preserves all newlines and indentation\" do\n        result = context_loader.send(:format_embedded_context,
      documents)\n\n        lines = result.split(\"\\n\")\n        content_lines =
      lines.select { |line| line.start_with?(\"        \") }\n\n        expect(content_lines[0]).to
      eq(\"        Line 1\")\n        expect(content_lines[1]).to eq(\"        \")
      \ # Empty line with indentation\n        expect(content_lines[2]).to eq(\"        Line
      3\")\n        expect(content_lines[3]).to eq(\"        \")  # Empty line with
      indentation\n        expect(content_lines[4]).to eq(\"        \")  # Empty line
      with indentation\n        expect(content_lines[5]).to eq(\"        Line 6\")\n
      \       # The final trailing newline creates an empty indented line, but the
      join doesn't add extra content\n        expect(content_lines.length).to eq(6)\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with document containing XML-like content\"
      do\n      let(:documents) do\n        [{\n          path: \"docs/xml.md\",\n
      \         content: \"# XML Example\\n\\n<tag attribute=\\\"value\\\">Content</tag>\"\n
      \       }]\n      end\n\n      it \"preserves XML-like content as-is\" do\n
      \       result = context_loader.send(:format_embedded_context, documents)\n\n
      \       expect(result).to include('        <tag attribute=\"value\">Content</tag>')\n
      \     end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"integration with ProjectSandbox\"
      do\n    context \"when using real ProjectSandbox\" do\n      let(:real_sandbox)
      { CodingAgentTools::Molecules::ProjectSandbox.new(project_root) }\n      let(:context_loader)
      { described_class.new(real_sandbox) }\n\n      before do\n        File.write(File.join(docs_dir,
      \"integration.md\"), \"# Integration Test\\n\\nReal sandbox test.\")\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"works with real ProjectSandbox instance\" do\n        result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n\n
      \       expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:files_loaded]).to
      eq(1)\n        expect(result[:context]).to include(\"# Integration Test\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"error handling and edge cases\" do\n
      \   let(:context_loader) { described_class.new(mock_sandbox) }\n\n    context
      \"when project_root is nil\" do\n      before do\n        allow(mock_sandbox).to
      receive(:project_root).and_return(nil)\n      end\n\n      it \"handles nil
      project root gracefully\" do\n        result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n\n
      \       expect(result[:success]).to be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to
      be_a(String)\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when docs directory path is
      invalid\" do\n      before do\n        allow(mock_sandbox).to receive(:project_root).and_return(\"/nonexistent/path\")\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"returns failure for nonexistent project root\" do\n
      \       result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n\n        expect(result[:success]).to
      be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to include(\"Docs directory not found\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"when file reading raises unexpected error\"
      do\n      before do\n        File.write(File.join(docs_dir, \"test.md\"), \"#
      Test\")\n\n        # Mock File.read to raise an unexpected error for this specific
      file\n        allow(File).to receive(:read).and_call_original\n        allow(File).to
      receive(:read).with(File.join(docs_dir, \"test.md\")).and_raise(Errno::EIO.new(\"I/O
      error\"))\n      end\n\n      it \"captures and reports the error\" do\n        result
      = context_loader.load_docs_context\n\n        expect(result[:success]).to be
      true\n        expect(result[:files_loaded]).to eq(0)\n        expect(result[:files_failed]).to
      eq(1)\n        expect(result[:failed_files].first[:error]).to include(\"I/O
      error\")\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"performance considerations\"
      do\n    let(:context_loader) { described_class.new(mock_sandbox) }\n\n    context
      \"with many files\" do\n      before do\n        # Create 50 small files to
      test performance\n        50.times do |i|\n          File.write(File.join(docs_dir,
      \"file_#{i.to_s.rjust(2, \"0\")}.md\"), \"# File #{i}\\n\\nContent for file
      #{i}.\")\n        end\n      end\n\n      it \"processes all files efficiently\"
      do\n        start_time = Time.now\n        result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n
      \       end_time = Time.now\n\n        expect(result[:success]).to be true\n
      \       expect(result[:files_loaded]).to eq(50)\n        expect(end_time - start_time).to
      be < 5.0  # Should complete within 5 seconds\n      end\n    end\n\n    context
      \"with large files\" do\n      before do\n        # Create a large file (approximately
      100KB)\n        large_content = \"# Large File\\n\\n\" + (\"Lorem ipsum dolor
      sit amet. \" * 4000)\n        File.write(File.join(docs_dir, \"large.md\"),
      large_content)\n      end\n\n      it \"handles large files correctly\" do\n
      \       result = context_loader.load_docs_context\n\n        expect(result[:success]).to
      be true\n        expect(result[:files_loaded]).to eq(1)\n        expect(result[:context].length).to
      be > 100_000\n        expect(result[:context]).to include(\"# Large File\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n  end\nend\n"
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/molecules/coverage_data_processor_spec.rb
    size: 6579
    content: |
      # frozen_string_literal: true

      require "spec_helper"
      require "tempfile"

      RSpec.describe CodingAgentTools::Molecules::CoverageDataProcessor do
        subject { described_class.new }

        let(:sample_raw_data) do
          {
            "RSpec" => {
              "coverage" => {
                "/test/lib/example.rb" => {
                  "lines" => [nil, 1, 0, 2, nil],
                  "branches" => {}
                },
                "/test/spec/example_spec.rb" => {
                  "lines" => [nil, 1, 1, 1],
                  "branches" => {}
                }
              },
              "timestamp" => 1_753_640_000
            },
            "MiniTest" => {
              "coverage" => {
                "/test/lib/example.rb" => {
                  "lines" => [nil, 0, 1, 1, nil],
                  "branches" => {}
                }
              },
              "timestamp" => 1_753_640_001
            }
          }
        end

        describe "#process_coverage_data" do
          context "with default options" do
            it "processes coverage data correctly" do
              result = subject.process_coverage_data(sample_raw_data)

              expect(result[:total_files]).to eq(1)  # Only lib file included by default
              expect(result[:processed_files]).to eq(1)
              expect(result[:frameworks]).to contain_exactly("RSpec", "MiniTest")
              expect(result[:timestamp]).to eq(1_753_640_001)  # Latest timestamp
            end

            it "filters spec files by default" do
              result = subject.process_coverage_data(sample_raw_data)

              file_paths = result[:file_coverage].keys
              expect(file_paths).not_to include("/test/spec/example_spec.rb")
              expect(file_paths).to include("/test/lib/example.rb")
            end

            it "combines coverage from multiple frameworks" do
              result = subject.process_coverage_data(sample_raw_data)

              file_data = result[:file_coverage]["/test/lib/example.rb"]
              coverage = file_data[:coverage_data]

              # Combined lines: [nil, 1, 1, 3, nil] -> 3 executable, 3 covered = 100%
              expect(coverage[:total_lines]).to eq(3)
              expect(coverage[:covered_lines]).to eq(3)
              expect(coverage[:coverage_percentage]).to eq(100.0)
            end
          end

          context "with custom include patterns" do
            let(:options) { {include_patterns: ["**/*.rb"], exclude_patterns: []} }

            it "includes all files matching pattern" do
              result = subject.process_coverage_data(sample_raw_data, options)

              expect(result[:total_files]).to eq(2)  # Both lib and spec files
              expect(result[:file_coverage].keys).to include("/test/spec/example_spec.rb")
            end
          end

          context "with custom exclude patterns" do
            let(:options) { {exclude_patterns: ["**/lib/**"]} }

            it "excludes files matching pattern" do
              result = subject.process_coverage_data(sample_raw_data, options)

              file_paths = result[:file_coverage].keys
              expect(file_paths).not_to include("/test/lib/example.rb")
            end
          end

          context "with malformed framework data" do
            let(:malformed_data) do
              {
                "RSpec" => "not a hash",
                "MiniTest" => {
                  "coverage" => {
                    "/test/lib/example.rb" => {
                      "lines" => [nil, 1, 0],
                      "branches" => {}
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            end

            it "handles malformed data gracefully" do
              result = subject.process_coverage_data(malformed_data)

              expect(result[:total_files]).to eq(1)
              expect(result[:frameworks]).to contain_exactly("RSpec", "MiniTest")
            end
          end
        end

        describe "#prioritize_under_covered_files" do
          let(:processed_data) do
            {
              file_coverage: {
                "/test/lib/low_coverage.rb" => {
                  coverage_data: {coverage_percentage: 60.0}
                },
                "/test/lib/high_coverage.rb" => {
                  coverage_data: {coverage_percentage: 95.0}
                }
              }
            }
          end

          it "separates files by threshold" do
            result = subject.prioritize_under_covered_files(processed_data, 80.0)

            expect(result[:under_covered_count]).to eq(1)
            expect(result[:under_covered_files]).to have_key("/test/lib/low_coverage.rb")
            expect(result[:well_covered_files]).to have_key("/test/lib/high_coverage.rb")
            expect(result[:threshold_used]).to eq(80.0)
          end

          it "validates threshold parameter" do
            expect do
              subject.prioritize_under_covered_files(processed_data, "invalid")
            end.to raise_error(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::ThresholdValidator::ValidationError)
          end
        end

        describe "#process_file" do
          let(:temp_file) { Tempfile.new(["coverage", ".json"]) }

          before do
            temp_file.write(JSON.generate(sample_raw_data))
            temp_file.rewind
          end

          after { temp_file.unlink }

          it "reads and processes SimpleCov file" do
            result = subject.process_file(temp_file.path)

            expect(result[:total_files]).to eq(1)
            expect(result[:processed_files]).to eq(1)
            expect(result[:frameworks]).to contain_exactly("RSpec", "MiniTest")
          end

          it "validates file structure" do
            invalid_file = Tempfile.new(["invalid", ".json"])
            invalid_file.write('{"invalid": "structure"}')
            invalid_file.rewind

            expect do
              subject.process_file(invalid_file.path)
            end.to raise_error(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::CoverageFileReader::InvalidFileError)

            invalid_file.unlink
          end
        end

        describe "private methods" do
          describe "#filter_file_paths" do
            let(:file_paths) do
              [
                "/test/lib/example.rb",
                "/test/spec/example_spec.rb",
                "/test/lib/another.rb",
                "/test/test/unit_test.rb"
              ]
            end

            it "prioritizes lib files in sort order" do
              filtered = subject.send(:filter_file_paths, file_paths, ["**/*.rb"], [])

              lib_files = filtered.select { |path| path.include?("/lib/") }
              filtered.reject { |path| path.include?("/lib/") }

              # Check that lib files come first
              expect(filtered.first(lib_files.length)).to eq(lib_files)
            end
          end

          describe "#combine_lines_data" do
            let(:coverage_arrays) do
              [
                [nil, 1, 0, 2],
                [nil, 0, 1, 1]
              ]
            end

            it "combines coverage arrays correctly" do
              result = subject.send(:combine_lines_data, coverage_arrays)

              expect(result).to eq([nil, 1, 1, 3])
            end

            it "handles arrays of different lengths" do
              arrays = [
                [nil, 1, 0],
                [nil, 0, 1, 1, 2]
              ]

              result = subject.send(:combine_lines_data, arrays)

              expect(result).to eq([nil, 1, 1, 1, 2])
            end
          end
        end
      end
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/molecules/doc_link_parser_spec.rb
    size: 15085
    content: |
      # frozen_string_literal: true

      require "spec_helper"
      require "tempfile"
      require "tmpdir"
      require "set"

      RSpec.describe CodingAgentTools::Molecules::DocLinkParser do
        let(:parser) { described_class.new(config_path) }
        let(:config_path) { nil }
        let(:mock_reference_extractor) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::FileReferenceExtractor) }
        let(:mock_path_resolver) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::PathResolver) }
        let(:mock_config_loader) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::DocsDependenciesConfigLoader) }
        let(:mock_config) { {} }

        before do
          allow(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::FileReferenceExtractor).to receive(:new).and_return(mock_reference_extractor)
          allow(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::PathResolver).to receive(:new).and_return(mock_path_resolver)
          allow(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::DocsDependenciesConfigLoader).to receive(:new).with(config_path).and_return(mock_config_loader)
          allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:load_config).and_return(mock_config)
        end

        describe "#initialize" do
          context "with no config path" do
            it "initializes with default config" do
              expect(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::DocsDependenciesConfigLoader).to receive(:new).with(nil)
              described_class.new
            end
          end

          context "with custom config path" do
            let(:config_path) { "/custom/config.yml" }

            it "uses provided config path" do
              expect(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::DocsDependenciesConfigLoader).to receive(:new).with(config_path)
              parser
            end
          end
        end

        describe "#parse_file_references" do
          context "when file does not exist" do
            it "returns empty array" do
              allow(File).to receive(:exist?).with("nonexistent.md").and_return(false)
              result = parser.parse_file_references("nonexistent.md", Set.new)
              expect(result).to eq([])
            end
          end

          context "when file exists" do
            let(:file_path) { "test.md" }
            let(:file_content) { "Some content with links" }
            let(:all_files) { Set.new(["docs/target.md", "other.md"]) }
            let(:raw_references) { ["docs/target.md", "other.md", "external.com", "#anchor"] }

            before do
              allow(File).to receive(:exist?).with(file_path).and_return(true)
              allow(File).to receive(:read).with(file_path).and_return(file_content)
              allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:extract_all_references).with(file_content).and_return(raw_references)
            end

            context "with internal links" do
              before do
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:external_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:anchor_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:internal_link?).and_return(true)
                allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:include_external_links?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:include_anchor_links?).and_return(false)

                # Stub all possible resolve_link calls
                allow(mock_path_resolver).to receive(:resolve_link).and_return("resolved")
                allow(mock_path_resolver).to receive(:resolve_link).with(file_path, "docs/target.md").and_return("docs/target.md")
                allow(mock_path_resolver).to receive(:resolve_link).with(file_path, "other.md").and_return("other.md")
              end

              it "processes internal links and returns existing files" do
                result = parser.parse_file_references(file_path, all_files)
                expect(result).to include("docs/target.md", "other.md")
              end
            end

            context "with external links when they are excluded" do
              before do
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:external_link?).with("external.com").and_return(true)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:external_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:anchor_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:internal_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:include_external_links?).and_return(false)
              end

              it "skips external links when configuration excludes them" do
                result = parser.parse_file_references(file_path, all_files)
                expect(result).not_to include("external.com")
              end
            end

            context "with external links when they are included" do
              before do
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:external_link?).with("external.com").and_return(true)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:external_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:anchor_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:internal_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:include_external_links?).and_return(true)
                allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:include_anchor_links?).and_return(false)

                allow(mock_path_resolver).to receive(:resolve_link).with(file_path, "external.com").and_return("external.com")
              end

              it "includes external links when configuration allows them" do
                # Note: external.com won't be in all_files so it won't be included in final result
                # but it should go through the processing pipeline
                result = parser.parse_file_references(file_path, all_files)
                expect(result).not_to include("external.com")  # Because it's not in all_files
              end
            end

            context "with anchor links when they are excluded" do
              before do
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:anchor_link?).with("#anchor").and_return(true)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:anchor_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:external_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:internal_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:include_anchor_links?).and_return(false)
              end

              it "skips anchor links when configuration excludes them" do
                result = parser.parse_file_references(file_path, all_files)
                expect(result).not_to include("#anchor")
              end
            end

            context "with anchor links when they are included" do
              before do
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:anchor_link?).with("#anchor").and_return(true)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:anchor_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:external_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_reference_extractor).to receive(:internal_link?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:include_external_links?).and_return(false)
                allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:include_anchor_links?).and_return(true)

                allow(mock_path_resolver).to receive(:resolve_link).with(file_path, "#anchor").and_return("#anchor")
              end

              it "includes anchor links when configuration allows them" do
                # Note: #anchor won't be in all_files so it won't be included in final result
                result = parser.parse_file_references(file_path, all_files)
                expect(result).not_to include("#anchor")  # Because it's not in all_files
              end
            end
          end
        end

        describe "#collect_documentation_files" do
          let(:file_patterns) { {"markdown" => "**/*.md", "workflow" => "**/*.wf.md"} }
          let(:exclude_patterns) { ["**/exclude_*.md"] }
          let(:skip_folders) { ["tmp", "node_modules"] }

          before do
            allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:get_file_patterns).with(mock_config).and_return(file_patterns)
            allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:get_exclude_patterns).with(mock_config).and_return(exclude_patterns)
            allow(mock_config_loader).to receive(:get_skip_folders).with(mock_config).and_return(skip_folders)
          end

          context "with matching files" do
            before do
              allow(Dir).to receive(:glob).with("**/*.md").and_return(["docs/test.md", "exclude_this.md", "tmp/hidden.md"])
              allow(Dir).to receive(:glob).with("**/*.wf.md").and_return(["workflows/test.wf.md", "node_modules/test.wf.md"])

              allow(File).to receive(:file?).and_return(true)
              allow(File).to receive(:fnmatch).with("**/exclude_*.md", "exclude_this.md").and_return(true)
              allow(File).to receive(:fnmatch).with("**/exclude_*.md", "docs/test.md").and_return(false)
              allow(File).to receive(:fnmatch).with("**/exclude_*.md", "tmp/hidden.md").and_return(false)
              allow(File).to receive(:fnmatch).with("**/exclude_*.md", "workflows/test.wf.md").and_return(false)
              allow(File).to receive(:fnmatch).with("**/exclude_*.md", "node_modules/test.wf.md").and_return(false)
            end

            it "collects files matching patterns while respecting exclusions and skip folders" do
              result = parser.collect_documentation_files

              expect(result).to include("docs/test.md")
              expect(result).to include("workflows/test.wf.md")
              expect(result).not_to include("exclude_this.md")  # Excluded by pattern
              expect(result).not_to include("tmp/hidden.md")  # In skip folder
              expect(result).not_to include("node_modules/test.wf.md")  # In skip folder
            end
          end

          context "with no matching files" do
            before do
              allow(Dir).to receive(:glob).and_return([])
            end

            it "returns empty set" do
              result = parser.collect_documentation_files
              expect(result).to be_empty
            end
          end

          context "with non-file entries" do
            before do
              allow(Dir).to receive(:glob).with("**/*.md").and_return(["docs/", "docs/test.md"])
              allow(Dir).to receive(:glob).with("**/*.wf.md").and_return([])

              allow(File).to receive(:file?).with("docs/").and_return(false)
              allow(File).to receive(:file?).with("docs/test.md").and_return(true)
              allow(File).to receive(:fnmatch).and_return(false)
            end

            it "only includes actual files" do
              result = parser.collect_documentation_files
              expect(result).to include("docs/test.md")
              expect(result).not_to include("docs/")
            end
          end
        end

        describe "#parse_with_context" do
          context "when file does not exist" do
            it "returns empty result structure" do
              allow(File).to receive(:exist?).with("nonexistent.md").and_return(false)
              result = parser.parse_with_context("nonexistent.md", Set.new)
              expect(result).to eq({markdown_links: [], context_refs: []})
            end
          end

          context "when file exists" do
            let(:file_path) { "test.md" }
