              modelVersion: "gemini-2.0-flash-lite",
              responseId: "SsVaaJKhOeaam9IP1fXesAg"
            }
          end

          it "extracts usage information from Google response" do
            result = described_class.parse(google_response)

            expect(result).to include(
              input_tokens: 2,
              output_tokens: 10,
              total_tokens: 12
            )
          end

          it "handles string keys in usage metadata" do
            response_with_string_keys = {
              "usage_metadata" => {
                "promptTokenCount" => 5,
                "candidatesTokenCount" => 15,
                "totalTokenCount" => 20
              }
            }

            result = described_class.parse(response_with_string_keys)

            expect(result).to include(
              input_tokens: 5,
              output_tokens: 15,
              total_tokens: 20
            )
          end

          it "calculates total tokens when not provided" do
            response_without_total = {
              usage_metadata: {
                promptTokenCount: 8,
                candidatesTokenCount: 12
              }
            }

            result = described_class.parse(response_without_total)

            expect(result).to include(
              input_tokens: 8,
              output_tokens: 12,
              total_tokens: 20
            )
          end

          it "handles missing usage metadata" do
            minimal_response = {}

            result = described_class.parse(minimal_response)

            expect(result).to include(
              input_tokens: 0,
              output_tokens: 0,
              total_tokens: 0
            )
          end

          it "extracts safety ratings" do
            response_with_safety = {
              usage_metadata: {promptTokenCount: 2, candidatesTokenCount: 3},
              safety_ratings: [
                {category: "HARM_CATEGORY_HARASSMENT", probability: "NEGLIGIBLE"}
              ]
            }

            result = described_class.parse(response_with_safety)

            expect(result[:safety_ratings]).to eq([
              {category: "HARM_CATEGORY_HARASSMENT", probability: "NEGLIGIBLE"}
            ])
          end

          it "extracts provider-specific metadata" do
            result = described_class.parse(google_response)

            expect(result[:provider_specific]).to include(
              prompt_token_details: google_response[:usageMetadata][:promptTokensDetails],
              candidate_token_details: google_response[:usageMetadata][:candidatesTokensDetails],
              model_version: "gemini-2.0-flash-lite",
              response_id: "SsVaaJKhOeaam9IP1fXesAg",
              avg_logprobs: -0.040499213337898257
            )
          end

          it "returns nil for provider_specific when no additional data" do
            minimal_response = {
              usage_metadata: {
                promptTokenCount: 2,
                candidatesTokenCount: 3
              }
            }

            result = described_class.parse(minimal_response)

            expect(result[:provider_specific]).to be_nil
          end

          it "handles cached tokens (future-proofing)" do
            response_with_cache = {
              usage_metadata: {
                promptTokenCount: 2,
                candidatesTokenCount: 3,
                cachedTokenCount: 5
              }
            }

            result = described_class.parse(response_with_cache)

            expect(result[:cached_tokens]).to eq(5)
          end
        end
      end
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/molecules/provider_usage_parsers/openai_usage_parser_spec.rb
    size: 2192
    content: |
      # frozen_string_literal: true

      require "spec_helper"
      require "coding_agent_tools/molecules/provider_usage_parsers/openai_usage_parser"

      RSpec.describe CodingAgentTools::Molecules::ProviderUsageParsers::OpenaiUsageParser do
        describe ".parse" do
          let(:openai_response) do
            {
              id: "test-id",
              object: "chat.completion",
              created: 1_750_780_428,
              model: "gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18",
              choices: [
                {
                  index: 0,
                  message: {
                    role: "assistant",
                    content: "Hello! How can I assist you today?",
                    refusal: nil,
                    annotations: []
                  },
                  logprobs: nil,
                  finish_reason: "stop"
                }
              ],
              usage_metadata: {
                prompt_tokens: 9,
                completion_tokens: 9,
                total_tokens: 18,
                prompt_tokens_details: {
                  cached_tokens: 0,
                  audio_tokens: 0
                },
                completion_tokens_details: {
                  reasoning_tokens: 0,
                  audio_tokens: 0,
                  accepted_prediction_tokens: 0,
                  rejected_prediction_tokens: 0
                }
              },
              service_tier: "default",
              system_fingerprint: "fp_34a54ae93c"
            }
          end

          it "extracts usage information from OpenAI response" do
            result = described_class.parse(openai_response)

            expect(result).to include(
              input_tokens: 9,
              output_tokens: 9,
              total_tokens: 18
            )
          end

          it "extracts cached tokens when available" do
            response_with_cache = {
              usage_metadata: {
                prompt_tokens: 9,
                completion_tokens: 9,
                total_tokens: 18,
                prompt_tokens_details: {
                  cached_tokens: 5
                }
              }
            }

            result = described_class.parse(response_with_cache)

            expect(result[:cached_tokens]).to eq(5)
          end

          it "extracts provider-specific metadata" do
            result = described_class.parse(openai_response)

            expect(result[:provider_specific]).to include(
              service_tier: "default",
              system_fingerprint: "fp_34a54ae93c",
              choice_index: 0
            )
          end
        end
      end
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/molecules/reflection/report_collector_spec.rb
    size: 28535
    content: "# frozen_string_literal: true\n\nrequire \"spec_helper\"\nrequire \"tempfile\"\nrequire
      \"tmpdir\"\n\nRSpec.describe CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Reflection::ReportCollector
      do\n  let(:collector) { described_class.new }\n  let(:temp_dir) { Dir.mktmpdir
      }\n\n  after do\n    FileUtils.rm_rf(temp_dir)\n  end\n\n  describe \"#collect_reports\"
      do\n    context \"when no reflection paths provided\" do\n      it \"returns
      failure with no paths\" do\n        result = collector.collect_reports([])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to be_failure\n        expect(result.error).to include(\"No
      valid reflection files found\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when handling
      glob patterns\" do\n      let(:reflection1) { File.join(temp_dir, \"20250101-123000-reflection.md\")
      }\n      let(:reflection2) { File.join(temp_dir, \"20250102-143000-reflection.md\")
      }\n      let(:non_reflection) { File.join(temp_dir, \"regular-doc.md\") }\n\n
      \     before do\n        File.write(reflection1, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Daily
      Reflection\n\n          **Date**: 2025-01-01\n          **Context**: First reflection\n\n
      \         ## What Went Well\n          - Completed major features\n          -
      Good team collaboration\n\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n          -
      Better time estimation\n          - More thorough testing\n\n          ## Key
      Learnings\n          - New framework patterns\n          - Team dynamics\n\n
      \         ## Action Items\n          - [ ] Improve testing process\n          -
      [ ] Update documentation\n        CONTENT\n\n        File.write(reflection2,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Project Reflection Notes\n\n          **Date**: 2025-01-02\n\n
      \         ## What Went Well\n          - Successful deployment\n          -
      No critical bugs\n\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n          - Communication
      could be better\n          - Need more automated tests\n\n          ## Key Learnings\n
      \         - Deployment process works well\n          - Monitoring is effective\n
      \       CONTENT\n\n        File.write(non_reflection, \"# Regular Documentation\\n\\nThis
      is not a reflection.\")\n      end\n\n      context \"when glob pattern matches
      reflection files\" do\n        it \"expands glob and collects valid reflections\"
      do\n          glob_pattern = File.join(temp_dir, \"*reflection*.md\")\n          result
      = collector.collect_reports([glob_pattern])\n\n          expect(result).to be_success\n
      \         expect(result.data[:reports]).to contain_exactly(reflection1, reflection2)\n
      \       end\n\n        it \"sorts the results\" do\n          glob_pattern =
      File.join(temp_dir, \"*reflection*.md\")\n          result = collector.collect_reports([glob_pattern])\n\n
      \         expect(result.data[:reports]).to eq([reflection1, reflection2])\n
      \       end\n      end\n\n      context \"when glob pattern matches no files\"
      do\n        it \"handles empty glob results\" do\n          no_match_pattern
      = File.join(temp_dir, \"nonexistent-*.md\")\n          result = collector.collect_reports([no_match_pattern])\n\n
      \         expect(result).to be_failure\n          expect(result.error).to include(\"No
      valid reflection files found\")\n        end\n      end\n\n      context \"when
      mixing direct paths and glob patterns\" do\n        it \"combines results from
      both\" do\n          direct_path = reflection1\n          glob_pattern = File.join(temp_dir,
      \"*reflection2*.md\")\n\n          # Rename reflection2 to match glob\n          reflection2_renamed
      = File.join(temp_dir, \"test-reflection2.md\")\n          File.rename(reflection2,
      reflection2_renamed)\n\n          result = collector.collect_reports([direct_path,
      glob_pattern])\n\n          expect(result).to be_success\n          expect(result.data[:reports]).to
      contain_exactly(reflection1, reflection2_renamed)\n        end\n\n        it
      \"removes duplicates\" do\n          glob_pattern = File.join(temp_dir, \"*reflection*.md\")\n
      \         direct_path = reflection1\n\n          result = collector.collect_reports([glob_pattern,
      direct_path])\n\n          expect(result).to be_success\n          expect(result.data[:reports]).to
      contain_exactly(reflection1, reflection2)\n        end\n      end\n    end\n\n
      \   context \"when handling direct file paths\" do\n      let(:reflection_file)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"team-reflection.md\") }\n\n      before do\n        File.write(reflection_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Team Reflection\n\n          **Context**: Sprint retrospective\n\n
      \         ## What Went Well\n          - Great team collaboration\n          -
      All features completed\n\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n          - Better
      planning\n          - More communication\n\n          ## Action Items\n          -
      Weekly check-ins\n          - Improved documentation\n        CONTENT\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"collects valid reflection files\" do\n        result = collector.collect_reports([reflection_file])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:reports]).to
      eq([reflection_file])\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when files don't
      exist\" do\n      it \"returns failure for non-existent files\" do\n        non_existent
      = File.join(temp_dir, \"missing.md\")\n        result = collector.collect_reports([non_existent])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to be_failure\n        expect(result.error).to include(\"File
      not found: #{non_existent}\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when files
      are not readable\" do\n      let(:unreadable_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"unreadable.md\")
      }\n\n      before do\n        File.write(unreadable_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          #
      Reflection\n          ## What Went Well\n          Something good\n        CONTENT\n
      \       File.chmod(0o000, unreadable_file)\n      end\n\n      after do\n        File.chmod(0o644,
      unreadable_file) # Restore permissions for cleanup\n      end\n\n      it \"skips
      unreadable files\" do\n        result = collector.collect_reports([unreadable_file])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to be_failure\n        expect(result.error).to include(\"Invalid
      reflection file\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when files are empty\"
      do\n      let(:empty_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"empty.md\") }\n\n      before
      do\n        File.write(empty_file, \"\")\n      end\n\n      it \"skips empty
      files\" do\n        result = collector.collect_reports([empty_file])\n\n        expect(result).to
      be_failure\n        expect(result.error).to include(\"Invalid reflection file\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"when files are not markdown\" do\n      let(:txt_file)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection.txt\") }\n\n      before do\n        File.write(txt_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Reflection\n          ## What Went Well\n          Something
      good\n        CONTENT\n      end\n\n      it \"skips non-markdown files\" do\n
      \       result = collector.collect_reports([txt_file])\n\n        expect(result).to
      be_failure\n        expect(result.error).to include(\"Invalid reflection file\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"when files don't have reflection content\"
      do\n      let(:regular_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"regular.md\") }\n\n      before
      do\n        File.write(regular_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Regular Documentation\n\n
      \         This is just regular documentation without reflection markers.\n          No
      reflection patterns here.\n        CONTENT\n      end\n\n      it \"skips files
      without reflection markers\" do\n        result = collector.collect_reports([regular_file])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to be_failure\n        expect(result.error).to include(\"Invalid
      reflection file\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with valid reflection
      files\" do\n      let(:reflection1) { File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection-alpha.md\")
      }\n      let(:reflection2) { File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection-beta.md\") }\n\n
      \     before do\n        File.write(reflection1, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Sprint
      Reflection\n\n          **Date**: 2025-01-01\n\n          ## What Went Well\n
      \         - Good progress on features\n          - Team worked well together\n\n
      \         ## What Could Be Improved\n          - Better time management\n          -
      More testing coverage\n\n          ## Key Learnings\n          - New patterns
      are effective\n          - Team communication improved\n\n          ## Action
      Items\n          - [ ] Update process documentation\n          - [ ] Schedule
      training session\n        CONTENT\n\n        File.write(reflection2, <<~CONTENT)\n
      \         # Project Reflection\n\n          **Context**: End of quarter review\n\n
      \         ## What Went Well\n          - All deliverables completed\n          -
      Quality remained high\n\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n          - Faster
      feedback cycles\n          - Better requirement clarity\n        CONTENT\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"returns successful result with sorted reports\" do\n        result
      = collector.collect_reports([reflection1, reflection2])\n\n        expect(result).to
      be_success\n        expect(result.data[:reports]).to eq([reflection1, reflection2])\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with mixed valid and invalid files\" do\n
      \     let(:valid_reflection) { File.join(temp_dir, \"valid-reflection.md\")
      }\n      let(:invalid_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"invalid.md\") }\n      let(:missing_file)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"missing.md\") }\n\n      before do\n        File.write(valid_reflection,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Valid Reflection\n\n          ## What Went Well\n          -
      Good progress\n\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n          - Better communication\n\n
      \         ## Action Items\n          - Follow up meeting\n        CONTENT\n\n
      \       File.write(invalid_file, \"# Regular Doc\\n\\nNot a reflection.\")\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"returns error when any file is invalid\" do\n        result
      = collector.collect_reports([valid_reflection, invalid_file, missing_file])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to be_failure\n        expect(result.error).to include(\"File
      not found: #{missing_file}\")\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"private
      methods\" do\n    describe \"#valid_reflection_file?\" do\n      let(:test_file)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"test.md\") }\n\n      context \"with valid extensions\"
      do\n        it \"accepts .md files\" do\n          File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n
      \           # Reflection\n            ## What Went Well\n            Good stuff\n
      \         CONTENT\n\n          expect(collector.send(:valid_reflection_file?,
      test_file)).to be true\n        end\n\n        it \"accepts .markdown files\"
      do\n          markdown_file = File.join(temp_dir, \"test.markdown\")\n          File.write(markdown_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n            # Reflection\n            ## What Went Well\n            Good
      stuff\n          CONTENT\n\n          expect(collector.send(:valid_reflection_file?,
      markdown_file)).to be true\n        end\n\n        it \"rejects other extensions\"
      do\n          txt_file = File.join(temp_dir, \"test.txt\")\n          File.write(txt_file,
      \"# Reflection\\n## What Went Well\\nGood stuff\")\n\n          expect(collector.send(:valid_reflection_file?,
      txt_file)).to be false\n        end\n      end\n\n      context \"with reflection
      content patterns\" do\n        it \"identifies files with 'What Went Well' pattern\"
      do\n          File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n            # Sprint Review\n
      \           ## What Went Well\n            Great progress\n            ## Action
      Items\n            Follow up\n          CONTENT\n\n          expect(collector.send(:valid_reflection_file?,
      test_file)).to be true\n        end\n\n        it \"identifies files with 'What
      Could Be Improved' pattern\" do\n          File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n
      \           # Review\n            ## What Could Be Improved\n            Better
      communication\n            ## Key Learnings\n            New patterns\n          CONTENT\n\n
      \         expect(collector.send(:valid_reflection_file?, test_file)).to be true\n
      \       end\n\n        it \"identifies files with reflection title\" do\n          File.write(test_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n            # Daily Reflection\n            **Date**: Today\n            Good
      day overall\n          CONTENT\n\n          expect(collector.send(:valid_reflection_file?,
      test_file)).to be true\n        end\n\n        it \"identifies files with context
      metadata\" do\n          File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n            # Review\n
      \           **Context**: Sprint retrospective\n            ## What Went Well\n
      \           Good session\n          CONTENT\n          expect(collector.send(:valid_reflection_file?,
      test_file)).to be true\n        end\n\n        it \"requires at least 2 reflection
      markers\" do\n          File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n            # Some
      Review\n            ## What Went Well\n            Only one marker here\n          CONTENT\n\n
      \         expect(collector.send(:valid_reflection_file?, test_file)).to be false\n
      \       end\n\n        it \"rejects files with no reflection patterns\" do\n
      \         File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n            # Regular Documentation\n
      \           This is just regular documentation.\n            No reflection patterns
      here.\n          CONTENT\n\n          expect(collector.send(:valid_reflection_file?,
      test_file)).to be false\n        end\n      end\n    end\n\n    describe \"#has_reflection_markers?\"
      do\n      it \"counts reflection patterns correctly\" do\n        content =
      <<~CONTENT\n          # Daily Reflection\n          **Date**: Today\n          ##
      What Went Well\n          Good progress\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n
      \         Better planning\n          ## Key Learnings\n          New insights\n
      \       CONTENT\n\n        expect(collector.send(:has_reflection_markers?, content)).to
      be true\n      end\n\n      it \"returns false for insufficient markers\" do\n
      \       content = <<~CONTENT\n          # Some Document\n          ## What Went
      Well\n          Only one reflection marker\n        CONTENT\n\n        expect(collector.send(:has_reflection_markers?,
      content)).to be false\n      end\n\n      it \"detects various reflection patterns
      case-insensitively\" do\n        content = <<~CONTENT\n          # Team reflection\n
      \         ## what went well\n          Great progress\n          ## What Could
      be improved\n          Better communication\n        CONTENT\n\n        expect(collector.send(:has_reflection_markers?,
      content)).to be true\n      end\n\n      it \"handles action items pattern\"
      do\n        content = <<~CONTENT\n          # Review\n          ## Action Items\n
      \         - Follow up tasks\n          **Context**: Sprint review\n        CONTENT\n\n
      \       expect(collector.send(:has_reflection_markers?, content)).to be true\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"detects key learnings pattern\" do\n        content
      = <<~CONTENT\n          # Session Review\n          ## Key Learnings\n          New
      insights gained\n          **Date**: 2025-01-01\n        CONTENT\n\n        expect(collector.send(:has_reflection_markers?,
      content)).to be true\n      end\n    end\n\n    describe \"#expand_glob_patterns\"
      do\n      let(:pattern_file1) { File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection-001.md\") }\n
      \     let(:pattern_file2) { File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection-002.md\") }\n      let(:direct_file)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"direct.md\") }\n\n      before do\n        File.write(pattern_file1,
      \"# Reflection\\n## What Went Well\\nGood\")\n        File.write(pattern_file2,
      \"# Reflection\\n## Key Learnings\\nLearned\")\n        File.write(direct_file,
      \"# Direct\\n## What Went Well\\nContent\")\n      end\n\n      it \"expands
      glob patterns correctly\" do\n        pattern = File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection-*.md\")\n
      \       result = collector.send(:expand_glob_patterns, [pattern, direct_file])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to contain_exactly(pattern_file1, pattern_file2, direct_file)\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"handles non-glob patterns as-is\" do\n        result
      = collector.send(:expand_glob_patterns, [direct_file])\n\n        expect(result).to
      eq([direct_file])\n      end\n\n      it \"removes duplicates from expanded
      patterns\" do\n        pattern = File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection-001.md\")\n
      \       result = collector.send(:expand_glob_patterns, [pattern, pattern_file1])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to eq([pattern_file1])\n      end\n\n      it \"handles
      empty glob results\" do\n        empty_pattern = File.join(temp_dir, \"nonexistent-*.md\")\n
      \       result = collector.send(:expand_glob_patterns, [empty_pattern])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to eq([])\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"edge
      cases and advanced scenarios\" do\n    context \"with complex glob patterns\"
      do\n      let(:nested_dir) { File.join(temp_dir, \"nested\") }\n      let(:nested_reflection)
      { File.join(nested_dir, \"deep-reflection.md\") }\n\n      before do\n        Dir.mkdir(nested_dir)\n
      \       File.write(nested_reflection, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Project Reflection\n
      \         **Context**: Deep nested reflection\n          ## What Went Well\n
      \         Found good patterns\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n          Better
      organization\n        CONTENT\n      end\n\n      it \"handles recursive glob
      patterns\" do\n        recursive_pattern = File.join(temp_dir, \"**\", \"*reflection*.md\")\n
      \       result = collector.collect_reports([recursive_pattern])\n\n        expect(result).to
      be_success\n        expect(result.data[:reports]).to include(nested_reflection)\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with file encoding and special characters\"
      do\n      let(:unicode_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"unicode-reflection.md\")
      }\n\n      before do\n        # Create file with unicode characters\n        File.write(unicode_file,
      <<~CONTENT, encoding: \"utf-8\")\n          # Daily Reflection \U0001F4DD\n
      \         **Date**: 2025-01-01\n          ## What Went Well ✅\n          Unicode
      content with émojis and àccents\n          ## What Could Be Improved \U0001F527\n
      \         Better handling of special characters\n        CONTENT\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"handles unicode content correctly\" do\n        result = collector.collect_reports([unicode_file])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:reports]).to
      include(unicode_file)\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with borderline reflection
      marker counts\" do\n      let(:borderline_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"borderline.md\")
      }\n\n      it \"accepts files with exactly 2 markers\" do\n        File.write(borderline_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Sprint Reflection\n          ## What Went Well\n          Exactly
      two markers here\n        CONTENT\n\n        result = collector.collect_reports([borderline_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n      end\n\n      it \"rejects files
      with only 1 marker\" do\n        File.write(borderline_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          #
      Some Document\n          ## What Went Well\n          Only one marker\n        CONTENT\n\n
      \       result = collector.collect_reports([borderline_file])\n        expect(result).to
      be_failure\n      end\n\n      it \"accepts files with many markers\" do\n        File.write(borderline_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Comprehensive Reflection\n          **Date**: Today\n
      \         **Context**: Full review\n          ## What Went Well\n          Multiple
      items\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n          Several areas\n          ##
      Key Learnings\n          Many insights\n          ## Action Items\n          Next
      steps\n        CONTENT\n\n        result = collector.collect_reports([borderline_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with
      file system edge cases\" do\n      it \"handles files with unusual extensions
      correctly\" do\n        weird_extension = File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection.MD\")
      # Uppercase\n        File.write(weird_extension, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Reflection\n
      \         ## What Went Well\n          Content here\n        CONTENT\n\n        result
      = collector.collect_reports([weird_extension])\n        expect(result).to be_success\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"rejects symlinks to non-existent files\" do\n        if
      File.respond_to?(:symlink) # Skip on systems without symlink support\n          broken_link
      = File.join(temp_dir, \"broken-link.md\")\n          non_existent = File.join(temp_dir,
      \"does-not-exist.md\")\n\n          begin\n            File.symlink(non_existent,
      broken_link)\n            result = collector.collect_reports([broken_link])\n
      \           expect(result).to be_failure\n          rescue NotImplementedError\n
      \           skip \"Symlinks not supported on this system\"\n          end\n
      \       else\n          skip \"Symlinks not available\"\n        end\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"with content validation edge cases\" do\n      let(:edge_case_file)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"edge.md\") }\n\n      it \"handles files with reflection
      patterns in code blocks\" do\n        File.write(edge_case_file, <<~CONTENT)\n
      \         # Documentation\n          \n          ```markdown\n          ## What
      Went Well\n          This is in a code block\n          ```\n          \n          ##
      What Could Be Improved\n          Real reflection content here\n          **Date**:
      Today\n        CONTENT\n\n        # Should still detect the real patterns outside
      code blocks\n        result = collector.collect_reports([edge_case_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n      end\n\n      it \"handles files
      with malformed reflection patterns\" do\n        File.write(edge_case_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Review\n          ##What Went Well (no space after
      ##)\n          Content\n          ## What Could Be improved (lowercase 'i')\n
      \         More content\n        CONTENT\n\n        # Should still detect case-insensitive
      patterns\n        result = collector.collect_reports([edge_case_file])\n        expect(result).to
      be_success\n      end\n\n      it \"handles very long lines in reflection files\"
      do\n        long_line = \"A\" * 5000  # Very long line\n        File.write(edge_case_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Reflection\n          ## What Went Well\n          #{long_line}\n
      \         ## Action Items\n          Regular content\n        CONTENT\n\n        result
      = collector.collect_reports([edge_case_file])\n        expect(result).to be_success\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    context \"with error handling scenarios\" do\n      it
      \"propagates file read errors\" do\n        error_file = File.join(temp_dir,
      \"error.md\")\n        File.write(error_file, \"Content\")\n\n        # Mock
      file read to raise an error\n        allow(File).to receive(:read).with(error_file,
      encoding: \"utf-8\").and_raise(StandardError, \"Read error\")\n\n        # The
      current implementation doesn't handle read errors, so it should raise\n        expect
      { collector.collect_reports([error_file]) }.to raise_error(StandardError, \"Read
      error\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"additional edge cases for coverage
      improvement\" do\n      let(:temp_dir) { Dir.mktmpdir }\n      let(:valid_reflection)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"valid.md\") }\n      let(:invalid_reflection) { File.join(temp_dir,
      \"invalid.md\") }\n      let(:missing_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"missing.md\")
      }\n\n      after do\n        FileUtils.rm_rf(temp_dir)\n      end\n\n      before
      do\n        # Create a valid reflection file\n        File.write(valid_reflection,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Sprint Reflection\n          **Date**: 2025-01-01\n
      \         ## What Went Well\n          Everything worked great\n          ##
      What Could Be Improved\n          Better communication\n        CONTENT\n\n
      \       # Create an invalid reflection file (regular markdown)\n        File.write(invalid_reflection,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Regular Documentation\n          This is just regular
      documentation.\n          No reflection patterns here.\n        CONTENT\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"handles combination of valid files, invalid files, and missing files
      with proper error accumulation\" do\n        result = collector.collect_reports([valid_reflection,
      invalid_reflection, missing_file])\n\n        expect(result).to be_failure\n
      \       expect(result.error).to include(\"Invalid reflection file: #{invalid_reflection}\")\n
      \       expect(result.error).to include(\"File not found: #{missing_file}\")\n
      \       # Should not include the valid file in error\n        expect(result.error).not_to
      include(valid_reflection)\n      end\n\n      it \"returns success when all
      files are valid reflections\" do\n        valid_reflection2 = File.join(temp_dir,
      \"valid2.md\")\n        File.write(valid_reflection2, <<~CONTENT)\n          #
      Team Reflection\n          **Context**: Sprint review\n          ## What Went
      Well\n          Great teamwork\n          ## Key Learnings\n          New insights\n
      \       CONTENT\n\n        result = collector.collect_reports([valid_reflection,
      valid_reflection2])\n\n        expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:reports]).to
      contain_exactly(valid_reflection, valid_reflection2)\n        expect(result.data[:reports]).to
      eq(result.data[:reports].sort)\n      end\n\n      it \"handles empty glob expansion
      gracefully\" do\n        empty_glob = File.join(temp_dir, \"nonexistent-pattern-*.md\")\n
      \       result = collector.collect_reports([empty_glob])\n\n        expect(result).to
      be_failure\n        expect(result.error).to eq(\"No valid reflection files found\")\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"handles mixed valid files and empty glob patterns\"
      do\n        empty_glob = File.join(temp_dir, \"nonexistent-*.md\")\n        result
      = collector.collect_reports([valid_reflection, empty_glob])\n\n        expect(result).to
      be_success\n        expect(result.data[:reports]).to eq([valid_reflection])\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"accumulates multiple different error types in a single
      call\" do\n        unreadable_file = File.join(temp_dir, \"unreadable.md\")\n
      \       empty_file = File.join(temp_dir, \"empty.md\")\n\n        # Create unreadable
      file\n        File.write(unreadable_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Reflection\n
      \         ## What Went Well\n          Good content\n        CONTENT\n        File.chmod(0o000,
      unreadable_file)\n\n        # Create empty file\n        File.write(empty_file,
      \"\")\n\n        begin\n          result = collector.collect_reports([invalid_reflection,
      missing_file, unreadable_file, empty_file])\n\n          expect(result).to be_failure\n
      \         # Should contain multiple error messages\n          error_msg = result.error\n
      \         expect(error_msg).to include(\"Invalid reflection file\")\n          expect(error_msg).to
      include(\"File not found\")\n        ensure\n          File.chmod(0o644, unreadable_file)
      # Restore permissions for cleanup\n        end\n      end\n\n      it \"processes
      files in deterministic order despite mixed file types\" do\n        # Test that
      sorting works correctly with mixed valid/invalid scenarios\n        reflection_a
      = File.join(temp_dir, \"a-reflection.md\")\n        reflection_z = File.join(temp_dir,
      \"z-reflection.md\")\n\n        File.write(reflection_a, <<~CONTENT)\n          #
      Reflection A\n          **Date**: Today\n          ## What Went Well\n          A
      went well\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n          A improvements\n        CONTENT\n\n
      \       File.write(reflection_z, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Reflection Z\n          **Context**:
      Final\n          ## What Went Well\n          Z went well\n          ## Key
      Learnings\n          Z learnings\n        CONTENT\n\n        result = collector.collect_reports([reflection_z,
      valid_reflection, reflection_a])\n\n        expect(result).to be_success\n        #
      Should be sorted alphabetically\n        expect(result.data[:reports]).to eq([reflection_a,
      valid_reflection, reflection_z])\n      end\n\n      it \"exercises all code
      paths in collect_reports method\" do\n        # This test specifically targets
      the exact flow through collect_reports\n        # to ensure all lines 15-38
      are covered\n\n        # Setup files that will exercise different code paths\n
      \       valid_file1 = File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection1.md\")\n        valid_file2
      = File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection2.md\")\n\n        File.write(valid_file1,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Daily Reflection\n          **Date**: 2025-01-01\n
      \         ## What Went Well\n          Great progress today\n          ## Action
      Items\n          Continue tomorrow\n        CONTENT\n\n        File.write(valid_file2,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Weekly Reflection\n          **Context**: Sprint review\n
      \         ## What Could Be Improved\n          Better planning\n          ##
      Key Learnings\n          Good insights\n        CONTENT\n\n        # Test successful
      path with multiple valid files\n        result = collector.collect_reports([valid_file1,
      valid_file2])\n\n        expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:reports]).to
      contain_exactly(valid_file1, valid_file2)\n        expect(result.data[:reports]).to
      eq(result.data[:reports].sort)\n      end\n\n      it \"exercises error accumulation
      and result failure paths\" do\n        # This specifically tests the error accumulation
      logic in lines 19-29\n        # and the failure result creation in lines 31-34\n\n
      \       invalid_file = File.join(temp_dir, \"not-reflection.md\")\n        missing_file1
      = File.join(temp_dir, \"missing1.md\")\n        missing_file2 = File.join(temp_dir,
      \"missing2.md\")\n\n        File.write(invalid_file, \"# Regular Doc\\nNot a
      reflection at all.\")\n\n        result = collector.collect_reports([invalid_file,
      missing_file1, missing_file2])\n\n        expect(result).to be_failure\n        expect(result.error).to
      include(\"Invalid reflection file: #{invalid_file}\")\n        expect(result.error).to
      include(\"File not found: #{missing_file1}\")\n        expect(result.error).to
      include(\"File not found: #{missing_file2}\")\n      end\n    end\n  end\nend\n"
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/molecules/reflection/synthesis_orchestrator_spec.rb
    size: 16920
    content: |
      # frozen_string_literal: true

      require "spec_helper"
      require "tempfile"
      require "tmpdir"
      require "ostruct"

      RSpec.describe CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Reflection::SynthesisOrchestrator do
        let(:orchestrator) { described_class.new }
        let(:mock_prompt_processor) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::PromptProcessor) }
        let(:temp_dir) { Dir.mktmpdir }
        let(:output_path) { File.join(temp_dir, "synthesis.md") }
        let(:system_prompt_path) { File.join(temp_dir, "system_prompt.txt") }

        before do
          allow(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::PromptProcessor).to receive(:new).and_return(mock_prompt_processor)
        end

        after do
          FileUtils.rm_rf(temp_dir)
        end

        describe "#initialize" do
          it "initializes with a prompt processor" do
            expect(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::PromptProcessor).to receive(:new)
            described_class.new
          end
        end

        describe "#synthesize_reflections" do
          let(:reflections) { [reflection_file1, reflection_file2] }
          let(:reflection_file1) { File.join(temp_dir, "reflection1.md") }
          let(:reflection_file2) { File.join(temp_dir, "reflection2.md") }
          let(:timestamp_info) { OpenStruct.new(valid?: true, from_date: "2024-01-01", to_date: "2024-01-31", days_covered: 30) }
          let(:model) { "test-model" }
          let(:format) { "markdown" }
          let(:force) { false }
          let(:debug) { false }

          let(:base_params) do
            {
              reflections: reflections,
              timestamp_info: timestamp_info,
              model: model,
              output_path: output_path,
              format: format,
              system_prompt_path: system_prompt_path,
              force: force,
              debug: debug
            }
          end

          before do
            # Create test reflection files
            File.write(reflection_file1, "# Reflection 1\n\nThis is the first reflection.")
            File.write(reflection_file2, "# Reflection 2\n\nThis is the second reflection.")

            # Create system prompt file (unless nil)
            if system_prompt_path
              File.write(system_prompt_path, "You are a helpful assistant.")
            end
          end

          context "with valid inputs" do
            it "successfully synthesizes reflections" do
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

              expect(result.success?).to be true
              expect(result.data[:output_path]).to eq(output_path)
              expect(result.data[:metrics][:reflections_count]).to eq(2)
              expect(result.data[:metrics][:execution_time]).to be >= 0
              expect(result.data[:synthesis_result]).to include("Reflection Synthesis")

              # Check that output file was created
              expect(File.exist?(output_path)).to be true
            end

            it "includes correct metrics in the result" do
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

              metrics = result.data[:metrics]
              expect(metrics[:reflections_count]).to eq(2)
              expect(metrics[:execution_time]).to be_a(Float)
              expect(metrics[:output_tokens]).to eq(100)
              expect(metrics[:cost]).to eq(0.01)
            end

            it "writes synthesis content to output file" do
              orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

              content = File.read(output_path)
              expect(content).to include("# Reflection Synthesis")
              expect(content).to include("Synthesis of 2 reflection notes")
            end
          end

          context "when output file already exists" do
            before do
              File.write(output_path, "Existing content")
            end

            context "without force flag" do
              it "returns failure result" do
                result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

                expect(result.success?).to be false
                expect(result.error).to include("Output file already exists")
                expect(result.error).to include("Use --force to overwrite")
              end

              it "does not overwrite existing file" do
                orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

                content = File.read(output_path)
                expect(content).to eq("Existing content")
              end
            end

            context "with force flag" do
              let(:force) { true }

              it "overwrites existing file successfully" do
                result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

                expect(result.success?).to be true
                content = File.read(output_path)
                expect(content).to include("# Reflection Synthesis")
              end
            end
          end

          context "when system prompt file is missing" do
            before do
              File.delete(system_prompt_path)
            end

            it "returns failure result" do
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

              expect(result.success?).to be false
              expect(result.error).to include("Could not load system prompt")
            end
          end

          context "when system prompt path is nil" do
            let(:system_prompt_path) { nil }
            let(:base_params_no_prompt) do
              {
                reflections: reflections,
                timestamp_info: timestamp_info,
                model: model,
                output_path: output_path,
                format: format,
                system_prompt_path: system_prompt_path,
                force: force,
                debug: debug
              }
            end

            it "returns failure result" do
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params_no_prompt)

              expect(result.success?).to be false
              expect(result.error).to include("Could not load system prompt")
            end
          end

          context "when output directory does not exist" do
            let(:output_path) { "/nonexistent/directory/output.md" }

            it "returns failure result" do
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

              expect(result.success?).to be false
              expect(result.error).to include("Could not write output file")
            end
          end

          context "when output path is not writable" do
            let(:output_path) { "/root/synthesis.md" }

            it "returns failure result" do
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

              expect(result.success?).to be false
              expect(result.error).to include("Could not write output file")
            end
          end

          context "with empty reflections array" do
            let(:reflections) { [] }

            it "processes successfully with zero reflections" do
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

              expect(result.success?).to be true
              expect(result.data[:metrics][:reflections_count]).to eq(0)
            end
          end

          context "with invalid timestamp info" do
            let(:timestamp_info) { OpenStruct.new(valid?: false) }

            it "still processes successfully without timestamp details" do
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

              expect(result.success?).to be true
              content = File.read(output_path)
              expect(content).not_to include("Analysis Period")
            end
          end

          context "with many reflections" do
            let(:reflections) { (1..10).map { |i| File.join(temp_dir, "reflection#{i}.md") } }

            before do
              reflections.each_with_index do |file, index|
                File.write(file, "# Reflection #{index + 1}\n\nContent for reflection #{index + 1}.")
              end
            end

            it "processes all reflections correctly" do
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

              expect(result.success?).to be true
              expect(result.data[:metrics][:reflections_count]).to eq(10)

              content = File.read(output_path)
              expect(content).to include("Synthesis of 10 reflection notes")
            end
          end
        end

        describe "private methods" do
          describe "#load_system_prompt" do
            context "when file exists and is readable" do
              let(:prompt_content) { "You are a helpful assistant for reflection synthesis." }

              before do
                File.write(system_prompt_path, prompt_content)
              end

              it "loads the system prompt content" do
                result = orchestrator.send(:load_system_prompt, system_prompt_path)
                expect(result).to eq(prompt_content)
              end
            end

            context "when file does not exist" do
              it "returns nil" do
                result = orchestrator.send(:load_system_prompt, "/nonexistent/prompt.txt")
                expect(result).to be_nil
              end
            end

            context "when path is nil" do
              it "returns nil" do
                result = orchestrator.send(:load_system_prompt, nil)
                expect(result).to be_nil
              end
            end

            context "when file exists but is not readable" do
              before do
                File.write(system_prompt_path, "content")
                allow(File).to receive(:read).with(system_prompt_path, encoding: "utf-8").and_raise(Errno::EACCES, "Permission denied")
              end

              it "returns nil and handles the error gracefully" do
                result = orchestrator.send(:load_system_prompt, system_prompt_path)
                expect(result).to be_nil
              end
            end

            context "with different encodings" do
              it "reads file with UTF-8 encoding" do
                File.write(system_prompt_path, "test content")
                expect(File).to receive(:read).with(system_prompt_path, encoding: "utf-8").and_call_original
                result = orchestrator.send(:load_system_prompt, system_prompt_path)
                expect(result).to eq("test content")
              end
            end
          end

          describe "#prepare_reflection_content" do
            let(:reflection_file1) { File.join(temp_dir, "reflection1.md") }
            let(:reflection_file2) { File.join(temp_dir, "reflection2.md") }
            let(:reflections) { [reflection_file1, reflection_file2] }
            let(:timestamp_info) { OpenStruct.new(valid?: true, from_date: "2024-01-01", to_date: "2024-01-31", days_covered: 30) }

            before do
              File.write(reflection_file1, "# First Reflection\n\nFirst reflection content.")
              File.write(reflection_file2, "# Second Reflection\n\nSecond reflection content.")
            end

            context "with valid timestamp info" do
              let(:timestamp_info) do
                OpenStruct.new(
                  valid?: true,
                  from_date: "2024-01-01",
                  to_date: "2024-01-31",
                  days_covered: 30
                )
              end

              it "includes timestamp information in content" do
                content = orchestrator.send(:prepare_reflection_content, reflections, timestamp_info)

                expect(content).to include("# Reflection Notes for Synthesis")
                expect(content).to include("**Analysis Period**: 2024-01-01 to 2024-01-31")
                expect(content).to include("**Duration**: 30 days")
                expect(content).to include("**Total Reflections**: 2")
              end
            end

            context "with invalid timestamp info" do
              let(:timestamp_info) { OpenStruct.new(valid?: false) }

              it "excludes timestamp information but includes reflection count" do
                content = orchestrator.send(:prepare_reflection_content, reflections, timestamp_info)

                expect(content).to include("# Reflection Notes for Synthesis")
                expect(content).not_to include("**Analysis Period**")
                expect(content).not_to include("**Duration**")
                expect(content).to include("**Total Reflections**: 2")
              end
            end

            context "with reflection files" do
              it "includes reflection content with proper headers" do
                content = orchestrator.send(:prepare_reflection_content, reflections, timestamp_info)

                expect(content).to include("## Reflection 1: reflection1.md")
                expect(content).to include("## Reflection 2: reflection2.md")
                expect(content).to include("**Source**: `#{reflection_file1}`")
                expect(content).to include("**Source**: `#{reflection_file2}`")
                expect(content).to include("# First Reflection")
                expect(content).to include("# Second Reflection")
              end

              it "includes file modification timestamps" do
                content = orchestrator.send(:prepare_reflection_content, reflections, timestamp_info)

                # Check that modification time is included (format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS)
                expect(content).to match(/\*\*Modified\*\*: \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}/)
              end

              it "separates reflections with horizontal rules" do
                content = orchestrator.send(:prepare_reflection_content, reflections, timestamp_info)

                expect(content.scan("---").length).to be >= 2  # At least separators between reflections
              end
            end

            context "when reflection file cannot be read" do
              before do
                allow(File).to receive(:read).with(reflection_file1, encoding: "utf-8").and_raise(Errno::ENOENT, "File not found")
                allow(File).to receive(:read).with(reflection_file2, encoding: "utf-8").and_return("# Second Reflection\n\nSecond reflection content.")
              end

              it "includes error message for unreadable files" do
                content = orchestrator.send(:prepare_reflection_content, reflections, timestamp_info)

                expect(content).to include("*Error reading reflection:")
                expect(content).to include("# Second Reflection")  # Other file should still be processed
              end
            end

            context "with empty reflections array" do
              let(:reflections) { [] }

              it "creates basic structure with zero reflections" do
                content = orchestrator.send(:prepare_reflection_content, reflections, timestamp_info)

                expect(content).to include("# Reflection Notes for Synthesis")
                expect(content).to include("**Total Reflections**: 0")
                expect(content).not_to include("## Reflection 1")
              end
            end

            context "reflection file paths and basenames" do
              let(:nested_reflection) { File.join(temp_dir, "nested", "deep", "reflection.md") }
              let(:reflections) { [nested_reflection] }

              before do
                FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(nested_reflection))
                File.write(nested_reflection, "# Nested Reflection\n\nContent.")
              end

              it "uses basename for reflection headers" do
                content = orchestrator.send(:prepare_reflection_content, reflections, timestamp_info)

                expect(content).to include("## Reflection 1: reflection.md")
                expect(content).to include("**Source**: `#{nested_reflection}`")
              end
            end
          end
        end

        describe "integration with dependencies" do
          it "initializes PromptProcessor correctly" do
            expect(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::PromptProcessor).to receive(:new).once
            described_class.new
          end
        end

        describe "error handling and edge cases" do
          let(:reflections) { [File.join(temp_dir, "reflection.md")] }
          let(:timestamp_info) { OpenStruct.new(valid?: true, from_date: "2024-01-01", to_date: "2024-01-31", days_covered: 30) }
          let(:base_params) do
            {
              reflections: reflections,
              timestamp_info: timestamp_info,
              model: "test-model",
              output_path: output_path,
              format: "markdown",
              system_prompt_path: system_prompt_path,
              force: false,
              debug: false
            }
          end

          before do
            File.write(reflections.first, "# Test Reflection\n\nContent.")
            File.write(system_prompt_path, "System prompt.")
          end

          context "when File.write raises an exception" do
            before do
              allow(File).to receive(:write).and_call_original
              allow(File).to receive(:write).with(output_path, anything).and_raise(IOError, "Disk full")
            end

            it "handles write errors gracefully" do
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)

              expect(result.success?).to be false
              expect(result.error).to include("Could not write output file: Disk full")
            end
          end

          context "with special characters in paths" do
            let(:special_output_path) { File.join(temp_dir, "output with spaces & symbols!.md") }
            let(:params_with_special_path) { base_params.merge(output_path: special_output_path) }

            it "handles paths with special characters" do
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**params_with_special_path)

              expect(result.success?).to be true
              expect(File.exist?(special_output_path)).to be true
            end
          end

          context "performance with large content" do
            let(:large_reflection) { File.join(temp_dir, "large_reflection.md") }
            let(:reflections) { [large_reflection] }

            before do
              # Create a reflection with substantial content
              large_content = "# Large Reflection\n\n" + ("This is a large reflection with lots of content. " * 1000)
              File.write(large_reflection, large_content)
            end

            it "processes large reflections efficiently" do
              start_time = Time.now
              result = orchestrator.synthesize_reflections(**base_params)
              end_time = Time.now

              expect(result.success?).to be true
              expect(end_time - start_time).to be < 5  # Should complete within 5 seconds
            end
          end
        end
      end

    content: "# frozen_string_literal: true\n\nrequire \"spec_helper\"\nrequire \"tempfile\"\nrequire
      \"tmpdir\"\nrequire \"date\"\n\nRSpec.describe CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Reflection::TimestampInferrer
      do\n  let(:inferrer) { described_class.new }\n  let(:temp_dir) { Dir.mktmpdir
      }\n\n  after do\n    FileUtils.rm_rf(temp_dir)\n  end\n\n  describe \"#infer_timestamp_range\"
      do\n    context \"when no reflection files provided\" do\n      it \"returns
      failure with empty array\" do\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to be_failure\n        expect(result.error).to include(\"No
      dates found in reflection files\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when
      files contain valid dates\" do\n      let(:file1) { File.join(temp_dir, \"20250101-120000-reflection.md\")
      }\n      let(:file2) { File.join(temp_dir, \"20250115-143000-reflection.md\")
      }\n      let(:file3) { File.join(temp_dir, \"20250110-reflection.md\") }\n\n
      \     before do\n        File.write(file1, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Daily Reflection\n
      \         **Date**: 2025-01-01\n          ## What Went Well\n          Good
      progress today\n        CONTENT\n\n        File.write(file2, <<~CONTENT)\n          #
      Project Reflection\n          **Date**: 2025-01-15\n          ## What Could
      Be Improved\n          Better time management\n        CONTENT\n\n        File.write(file3,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Sprint Reflection\n          Date: 2025-01-10\n          ##
      Key Learnings\n          Important insights gained\n        CONTENT\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"returns success with correct date range\" do\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([file1,
      file2, file3])\n\n        expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 1, 1))\n        expect(result.data[:to_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025,
      1, 15))\n        expect(result.data[:days_covered]).to eq(15) # Jan 1 to Jan
      15 inclusive\n        expect(result.data[:total_dates]).to be >= 3\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"handles files with multiple dates correctly\" do\n        File.write(file1,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Reflection with multiple dates\n          **Date**:
      2025-01-01\n          ## What Went Well\n          Good progress on 2025-01-02\n
      \         ## Key Learnings\n          Date: 2025-01-03\n        CONTENT\n\n
      \       result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([file1])\n\n        expect(result).to
      be_success\n        expect(result.data[:total_dates]).to be >= 3\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"when files have dates only in filenames\" do\n      let(:file1)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"2025-01-05-team-reflection.md\") }\n      let(:file2)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection-20250110.md\") }\n\n      before do\n        File.write(file1,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Team Reflection\n          ## What Went Well\n          Great
      collaboration\n        CONTENT\n\n        File.write(file2, <<~CONTENT)\n          #
      Sprint Reflection\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n          Better planning\n
      \       CONTENT\n      end\n\n      it \"extracts dates from filenames successfully\"
      do\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([file1, file2])\n\n        expect(result).to
      be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025, 1,
      5))\n        expect(result.data[:to_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025, 1, 10))\n        expect(result.data[:days_covered]).to
      eq(6)\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when files have mixed date formats\"
      do\n      let(:file1) { File.join(temp_dir, \"20250101-reflection.md\") }\n
      \     let(:file2) { File.join(temp_dir, \"general-reflection.md\") }\n\n      before
      do\n        File.write(file1, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Reflection\n          ##
      What Went Well\n          Good progress\n        CONTENT\n\n        File.write(file2,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # General Reflection\n          **Date**: 2025-01-20\n
      \         # Another date reference: 2025-01-25\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n
      \         Better communication\n        CONTENT\n      end\n\n      it \"handles
      mixed date sources correctly\" do\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([file1,
      file2])\n\n        expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 1, 1))\n        expect(result.data[:to_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025,
      1, 25))\n        expect(result.data[:days_covered]).to eq(25)\n      end\n    end\n\n
      \   context \"when files contain no recognizable dates\" do\n      let(:file1)
      { File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection-general.md\") }\n      let(:file2) { File.join(temp_dir,
      \"notes.md\") }\n\n      before do\n        File.write(file1, <<~CONTENT)\n
      \         # General Reflection\n          ## What Went Well\n          Good
      things happened\n        CONTENT\n\n        File.write(file2, <<~CONTENT)\n
      \         # Project Notes\n          ## What Could Be Improved\n          Various
      improvements needed\n        CONTENT\n      end\n\n      it \"returns failure
      when no dates found\" do\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([file1,
      file2])\n\n        expect(result).to be_failure\n        expect(result.error).to
      include(\"No dates found in reflection files\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context
      \"when files are unreadable\" do\n      let(:readable_file) { File.join(temp_dir,
      \"20250101-readable.md\") }\n      let(:unreadable_file) { File.join(temp_dir,
      \"20250102-unreadable.md\") }\n\n      before do\n        File.write(readable_file,
      \"# Reflection\\n**Date**: 2025-01-01\")\n        File.write(unreadable_file,
      \"# Reflection\\n**Date**: 2025-01-02\")\n        File.chmod(0o000, unreadable_file)\n
      \     end\n\n      after do\n        File.chmod(0o644, unreadable_file) # Restore
      for cleanup\n      end\n\n      it \"uses filename dates when content is unreadable\"
      do\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([readable_file, unreadable_file])\n\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 1, 1))\n        expect(result.data[:to_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025,
      1, 2))\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when single file provided\" do\n
      \     let(:single_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"20250301-single.md\") }\n\n
      \     before do\n        File.write(single_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Single
      Reflection\n          **Date**: 2025-03-01\n          ## What Went Well\n          Solo
      reflection\n        CONTENT\n      end\n\n      it \"handles single file correctly\"
      do\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([single_file])\n\n        expect(result).to
      be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025, 3,
      1))\n        expect(result.data[:to_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025, 3, 1))\n        expect(result.data[:days_covered]).to
      eq(1)\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"date extraction patterns\"
      do\n    let(:test_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"test-file.md\") }\n\n    describe
      \"filename pattern recognition\" do\n      it \"recognizes YYYY-MM-DD format\"
      do\n        filename_file = File.join(temp_dir, \"2025-01-15-reflection.md\")\n
      \       File.write(filename_file, \"# Test\")\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([filename_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 1, 15))\n      end\n\n      it \"recognizes YYYYMMDD format\"
      do\n        filename_file = File.join(temp_dir, \"20250115-reflection.md\")\n
      \       File.write(filename_file, \"# Test\")\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([filename_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 1, 15))\n      end\n\n      it \"recognizes YYYYMMDD without
      separators\" do\n        filename_file = File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection-20250115.md\")\n
      \       File.write(filename_file, \"# Test\")\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([filename_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 1, 15))\n      end\n    end\n\n    describe \"content pattern
      recognition\" do\n      it \"recognizes **Date**: pattern\" do\n        File.write(test_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Test Reflection\n          **Date**: 2025-02-10\n          ##
      Content\n          Some content\n        CONTENT\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 2, 10))\n      end\n\n      it \"recognizes Date: pattern\"
      do\n        File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Test Reflection\n
      \         Date: 2025-02-11\n          ## Content\n          Some content\n        CONTENT\n\n
      \       result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n        expect(result).to
      be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025, 2,

      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Daily Reflection 2025-02-12\n          ## Content\n
      \         Some content\n        CONTENT\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 2, 12))\n      end\n\n      it \"recognizes case variations\"
      do\n        File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Test Reflection\n
      \         **date**: 2025-02-13\n          ## Content\n          Some content\n
      \       CONTENT\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 2, 13))\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"edge cases
      and validation\" do\n    let(:test_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"edge-case.md\")
      }\n\n    describe \"date validation\" do\n      it \"rejects invalid dates\"
      do\n        File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Test\n          **Date**:
      2025-13-40  # Invalid month and day\n          Some content\n        CONTENT\n\n
      \       result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n        expect(result).to
      be_failure\n      end\n\n      it \"rejects dates outside reasonable range\"
      do\n        File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Test  \n          **Date**:
      1999-01-01  # Too old\n          Some content\n        CONTENT\n\n        result
      = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n        expect(result).to be_failure\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"rejects dates too far in future\" do\n        File.write(test_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Test\n          **Date**: 2031-01-01  # Too far in
      future\n          Some content\n        CONTENT\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_failure\n      end\n\n      it \"accepts edge case
      valid dates\" do\n        File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Test\n
      \         **Date**: 2000-01-01  # Minimum year\n          Some content\n        CONTENT\n\n
      \       result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n        expect(result).to
      be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to eq(Date.new(2000, 1,
      1))\n      end\n\n      it \"accepts leap year dates\" do\n        File.write(test_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Test\n          **Date**: 2024-02-29  # Leap year\n
      \         Some content\n        CONTENT\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2024, 2, 29))\n      end\n    end\n\n    describe \"duplicate date
      handling\" do\n      it \"handles duplicate dates correctly\" do\n        File.write(test_file,
      <<~CONTENT)\n          # Test Reflection  \n          **Date**: 2025-03-15\n
      \         ## Content\n          Date: 2025-03-15  # Same date repeated\n          More
      content on 2025-03-15\n        CONTENT\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:total_dates]).to
      eq(1) # Should deduplicate\n      end\n    end\n\n    describe \"malformed content

      \"\")\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n        expect(result).to
      be_failure\n      end\n\n      it \"handles files with only whitespace\" do\n
      \       File.write(test_file, \"   \\n\\n  \\t  \\n\")\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_failure\n      end\n\n      it \"handles binary
      files gracefully\" do\n        # Write some binary content\n        File.binwrite(test_file,
      \"\\x00\\x01\\x02\\x03\")\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_failure\n      end\n    end\n\n    describe \"unicode
      and encoding\" do\n      it \"handles unicode content correctly\" do\n        File.write(test_file,
      <<~CONTENT, encoding: \"utf-8\")\n          # Reflection with émojis \U0001F4DD\n
      \         **Date**: 2025-04-01\n          ## What Went Well ✅\n          Unicode
      content with àccents\n        CONTENT\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 4, 1))\n      end\n    end\n\n    describe \"multiple patterns
      in single line\" do\n      it \"extracts first date when multiple dates in single
      line\" do\n        File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Test\n          **Date**:
      2025-05-01 and reference to 2025-05-15\n          ## Content\n          Some
      content\n        CONTENT\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 5, 1))\n        expect(result.data[:to_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025,
      5, 1))\n        expect(result.data[:total_dates]).to eq(1)\n      end\n\n      it
      \"extracts dates from different lines\" do\n        File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n
      \         # Test\n          **Date**: 2025-05-01\n          ## Content\n          Reference
      to date: 2025-05-15\n        CONTENT\n\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to
      eq(Date.new(2025, 5, 1))\n        expect(result.data[:to_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025,
      5, 15))\n        expect(result.data[:total_dates]).to eq(2)\n      end\n    end\n\n
      \   describe \"partial date matches\" do\n      it \"ignores incomplete date
      patterns\" do\n        File.write(test_file, <<~CONTENT)\n          # Test\n
      \         Year 2025 and month 01 but no complete date\n          ## Content
      \ \n          **Date**: 2025-06-01  # This should be found\n        CONTENT\n\n
      \       result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([test_file])\n        expect(result).to
      be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025, 6,
      1))\n        expect(result.data[:total_dates]).to eq(1)\n      end\n    end\n
      \ end\n\n  describe \"file system edge cases\" do\n    context \"when non-existent
      files provided\" do\n      it \"handles missing files gracefully\" do\n        missing_file
      = File.join(temp_dir, \"missing.md\")\n\n        # Should not raise an error,
      but use empty content\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([missing_file])\n
      \       expect(result).to be_failure\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when
      symlinks provided\" do\n      let(:target_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"target.md\")
      }\n      let(:symlink_file) { File.join(temp_dir, \"symlink.md\") }\n\n      before
      do\n        File.write(target_file, \"# Test\\n**Date**: 2025-07-01\")\n        if
      File.respond_to?(:symlink)\n          begin\n            File.symlink(target_file,
      symlink_file)\n          rescue NotImplementedError\n            skip \"Symlinks
      not supported\"\n          end\n        else\n          skip \"Symlinks not
      available\"\n        end\n      end\n\n      it \"follows symlinks correctly\"
      do\n        result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([symlink_file])\n        expect(result).to
      be_success\n        expect(result.data[:from_date]).to eq(Date.new(2025, 7,
      1))\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"performance considerations\"
      do\n    it \"handles large number of files efficiently\" do\n      # Create
      many small files\n      files = []\n      50.times do |i|\n        file_path
      = File.join(temp_dir, \"reflection-#{i + 1}.md\")\n        File.write(file_path,
      \"# Reflection #{i + 1}\\n**Date**: 2025-01-#{(i % 28) + 1}\")\n        files
      << file_path\n      end\n\n      start_time = Time.now\n      result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range(files)\n
      \     end_time = Time.now\n\n      expect(result).to be_success\n      expect(end_time
      - start_time).to be < 5.0 # Should complete within 5 seconds\n    end\n\n    it
      \"handles files with large content efficiently\" do\n      large_content = \"#
      Large Reflection\\n**Date**: 2025-08-01\\n\" + (\"Content line\\n\" * 10_000)\n
      \     large_file = File.join(temp_dir, \"large.md\")\n      File.write(large_file,
      large_content)\n\n      start_time = Time.now\n      result = inferrer.infer_timestamp_range([large_file])\n
      \     end_time = Time.now\n\n      expect(result).to be_success\n      expect(end_time
      - start_time).to be < 2.0 # Should complete within 2 seconds\n    end\n  end\nend\n"
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/molecules/report_formatter_spec.rb
    size: 20212
    content: |
      # frozen_string_literal: true

      require "spec_helper"
      require "tempfile"

      RSpec.describe CodingAgentTools::Molecules::ReportFormatter do
        subject { described_class.new }

        let(:sample_coverage_results) do
          method_needing_tests = instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Models::MethodCoverage,
            name: "low_coverage_method",
            start_line: 5,
            end_line: 10,
            coverage_percentage: 0.0,
            uncovered_lines: [6, 7, 8],
            uncovered_lines_compact: "6..8",
            needs_tests?: true)
          allow(method_needing_tests).to receive(:to_h).with(format: :compact).and_return({
            name: "low_coverage_method",
            start_line: 5,
            end_line: 10,
            coverage_percentage: 0.0,
            uncovered_lines: "6..8"
          })
          allow(method_needing_tests).to receive(:to_h).with(format: :verbose).and_return({
            name: "low_coverage_method",
            start_line: 5,
            end_line: 10,
            coverage_percentage: 0.0,
            uncovered_lines: [6, 7, 8]
          })

          low_coverage_file = instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Models::CoverageResult,
            file_path: "/test/lib/low_coverage.rb",
            relative_path: "lib/low_coverage.rb",
            total_lines: 20,
            covered_lines: 10,
            coverage_percentage: 50.0,
            uncovered_lines_count: 10,
            methods: [method_needing_tests])
          allow(low_coverage_file).to receive(:under_threshold?).with(80.0).and_return(true)
          allow(low_coverage_file).to receive(:to_h).with(format: :compact).and_return({
            file_path: "/test/lib/low_coverage.rb",
            relative_path: "lib/low_coverage.rb",
            coverage_percentage: 50.0,
            total_lines: 20,
            covered_lines: 10
          })
          allow(low_coverage_file).to receive(:to_h).with(format: :verbose).and_return({
            file_path: "/test/lib/low_coverage.rb",
            relative_path: "lib/low_coverage.rb",
            coverage_percentage: 50.0,
            total_lines: 20,
            covered_lines: 10
          })

          high_coverage_file = instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Models::CoverageResult,
            file_path: "/test/lib/high_coverage.rb",
            relative_path: "lib/high_coverage.rb",
            total_lines: 15,
            covered_lines: 14,
            coverage_percentage: 93.3,
            uncovered_lines_count: 1,
            methods: [])
          allow(high_coverage_file).to receive(:under_threshold?).with(80.0).and_return(false)
          allow(high_coverage_file).to receive(:to_h).with(format: :compact).and_return({
            file_path: "/test/lib/high_coverage.rb",
            relative_path: "lib/high_coverage.rb",
            coverage_percentage: 93.3,
            total_lines: 15,
            covered_lines: 14
          })
          allow(high_coverage_file).to receive(:to_h).with(format: :verbose).and_return({
            file_path: "/test/lib/high_coverage.rb",
            relative_path: "lib/high_coverage.rb",
            coverage_percentage: 93.3,
            total_lines: 15,
            covered_lines: 14
          })

          [low_coverage_file, high_coverage_file]
        end

        let(:sample_analysis_result) do
          analysis_result = instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Models::CoverageAnalysisResult,
            files: sample_coverage_results,
            under_covered_files: [sample_coverage_results.first],
            total_files: 2,
            threshold: 80.0,
