      \     expect(result).to eq(expected_path)\n    end\n\n    it \"handles custom
      system prompt override\" do\n      custom_path = \"/custom/prompt.md\"\n\n      expect(@mock_prompt_combiner).to
      receive(:select_system_prompt).with(\"security\", custom_path).and_return(custom_path)\n\n
      \     result = prompt_builder.select_system_prompt(\"security\", custom_path)\n
      \     expect(result).to eq(custom_path)\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#build_immediate_prompt\"
      do\n    let(:context) do\n      CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewContext.new(\n
      \       mode: \"auto\",\n        documents: [\n          {type: \"blueprint\",
      path: \"README.md\", content: \"# Project Blueprint\"}\n        ],\n        loaded_at:
      Time.now\n      )\n    end\n\n    let(:mock_immediate_prompt) do\n      CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewPrompt.new(\n
      \       session_id: \"temp-123\",\n        focus_areas: [\"Code quality, architecture,
      security, performance\"],\n        system_prompt_path: \"prompts/security.md\",\n
      \       combined_content: \"Immediate prompt content\",\n        metadata: {generated_at:
      Time.now.iso8601}\n      )\n    end\n\n    before do\n      allow(@mock_prompt_combiner).to
      receive(:build_prompt).and_return(mock_immediate_prompt)\n      allow(Dir).to
      receive(:tmpdir).and_return(\"/tmp\")\n    end\n\n    it \"builds prompt for
      immediate use without saving\" do\n      target_content = \"function test()
      { return true; }\"\n\n      result = prompt_builder.build_immediate_prompt(\"security\",
      target_content, context)\n\n      expect(@mock_prompt_combiner).to have_received(:build_prompt)
      do |session, content, ctx, focus|\n        expect(session.session_id).to start_with(\"temp-\")\n
      \       expect(session.session_name).to eq(\"temp\")\n        expect(session.directory_path).to
      eq(\"/tmp\")\n        expect(session.focus).to eq(\"security\")\n        expect(session.target).to
      eq(\"immediate\")\n        expect(content).to eq(target_content)\n        expect(ctx).to
      eq(context)\n        expect(focus).to eq(\"security\")\n      end\n\n      expect(result).to
      eq(\"Immediate prompt content\")\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#get_prompt_stats\"
      do\n    let(:mock_prompt) do\n      CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewPrompt.new(\n
      \       session_id: \"test-session\",\n        focus_areas: [\"Architecture
      compliance (see docs/architecture.md)\", \"Code quality, architecture, security,
      performance\"],\n        system_prompt_path: \"prompts/architecture.md\",\n
      \       combined_content: \"---\\nfocus: architecture security\\ncustom: value\\n---\\nContent
      with some words here\",\n        metadata: {generated_at: Time.now.iso8601}\n
      \     )\n    end\n\n    # No mocking needed - multi_focus? is a real method\n\n
      \   it \"returns comprehensive prompt statistics\" do\n      stats = prompt_builder.get_prompt_stats(mock_prompt)\n\n
      \     expect(stats).to eq({\n        size_bytes: mock_prompt.content_size,\n
      \       word_count: mock_prompt.word_count,\n        multi_focus: true,\n        primary_focus:
      \"Architecture compliance (see docs/architecture.md)\",\n        focus_count:
      2,\n        has_frontmatter: true,\n        session_id: \"test-session\"\n      })\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"with simple prompt\" do\n      let(:simple_prompt)
      do\n        CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewPrompt.new(\n          session_id:
      \"simple-session\",\n          focus_areas: [\"Code quality, architecture, security,
      performance\"],\n          system_prompt_path: \"prompts/performance.md\",\n
      \         combined_content: \"Simple content\",\n          metadata: {}\n        )\n
      \     end\n\n      # No mocking needed - multi_focus? is a real method\n\n      it
      \"returns stats for simple prompt\" do\n        stats = prompt_builder.get_prompt_stats(simple_prompt)\n\n
      \       expect(stats[:multi_focus]).to be false\n        expect(stats[:focus_count]).to
      eq(1)\n        expect(stats[:has_frontmatter]).to be false\n      end\n    end\n
      \ end\n\n  # Edge case and comprehensive testing scenarios\n  describe \"edge
      case handling\", :content_type_edge_cases do\n    let(:session) do\n      CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewSession.new(\n
      \       session_id: \"edge-case-session\",\n        session_name: \"edge\",\n
      \       timestamp: Time.now.iso8601,\n        directory_path: session_dir,\n
      \       focus: \"security\",\n        target: \"src/\",\n        context_mode:
      \"auto\",\n        metadata: {}\n      )\n    end\n\n    let(:context) do\n
      \     CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewContext.new(\n        mode: \"auto\",\n
      \       documents: [],\n        loaded_at: Time.now\n      )\n    end\n\n    context
      \"with edge case content types\" do\n      let(:edge_case_target) do\n        CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewTarget.new(\n
      \         type: \"file_pattern\",\n          target_spec: \"src/*.rb\",\n          resolved_paths:
      [\"src/app.rb\"],\n          content_type: \"yaml\",\n          size_info: {files:
      1, lines: 10}\n        )\n      end\n\n      it \"raises error for unsupported
      content types\" do\n        expect do\n          prompt_builder.build_review_prompt(session,
      edge_case_target, context)\n        end.to raise_error(\"Unknown content type:
      yaml\")\n      end\n\n      it \"handles empty content type gracefully\" do\n
      \       empty_target = CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewTarget.new(\n          type:
      \"file_pattern\",\n          target_spec: \"src/*.rb\",\n          resolved_paths:
      [],\n          content_type: \"\",\n          size_info: {}\n        )\n\n        expect
      do\n          prompt_builder.build_review_prompt(session, empty_target, context)\n
      \       end.to raise_error(\"Unknown content type: \")\n      end\n\n      it
      \"handles nil content type\" do\n        nil_target = CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewTarget.new(\n
      \         type: \"file_pattern\",\n          target_spec: \"src/*.rb\",\n          resolved_paths:
      [],\n          content_type: nil,\n          size_info: {}\n        )\n\n        expect
      do\n          prompt_builder.build_review_prompt(session, nil_target, context)\n
      \       end.to raise_error(\"Unknown content type: \")\n      end\n    end\n
      \ end\n\n  describe \"file system error handling\", :file_system_errors do\n
      \   let(:session) do\n      CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewSession.new(\n
      \       session_id: \"error-session\",\n        session_name: \"error\",\n        timestamp:
      Time.now.iso8601,\n        directory_path: session_dir,\n        focus: \"architecture\",\n
      \       target: \"src/\",\n        context_mode: \"auto\",\n        metadata:
      {}\n      )\n    end\n\n    let(:target) do\n      CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewTarget.new(\n
      \       type: \"file_pattern\",\n        target_spec: \"src/*.rb\",\n        resolved_paths:
      [\"src/app.rb\"],\n        content_type: \"xml\",\n        size_info: {files:
      1, lines: 10}\n      )\n    end\n\n    let(:context) do\n      CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewContext.new(\n
      \       mode: \"auto\",\n        documents: [],\n        loaded_at: Time.now\n
      \     )\n    end\n\n    context \"when input file is corrupted\" do\n      before
      do\n        # Create a corrupted file\n        File.write(File.join(session_dir,
      \"input.xml\"), \"\\x00\\x01\\x02corrupted\")\n\n        allow(@mock_file_reader).to
      receive(:read).and_return({\n          success: false,\n          error: \"File
      contains invalid characters\"\n        })\n      end\n\n      it \"handles corrupted
      file gracefully\" do\n        expect do\n          prompt_builder.build_review_prompt(session,
      target, context)\n        end.to raise_error(\"Failed to read target content:
      File contains invalid characters\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when
      session directory is inaccessible\" do\n      before do\n        allow(@mock_file_reader).to
      receive(:read).and_return({\n          success: false,\n          error: \"Permission
      denied\"\n        })\n      end\n\n      it \"handles permission errors appropriately\"
      do\n        expect do\n          prompt_builder.build_review_prompt(session,
      target, context)\n        end.to raise_error(\"Failed to read target content:
      Permission denied\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when file system is
      full\" do\n      before do\n        allow(@mock_file_reader).to receive(:read).and_return({\n
      \         success: true,\n          content: \"valid content\"\n        })\n\n
      \       allow(@mock_prompt_combiner).to receive(:build_prompt).and_return(\n
      \         CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewPrompt.new(\n            session_id:
      \"error-session\",\n            focus_areas: [\"Architecture\"],\n            system_prompt_path:
      \"prompts/architecture.md\",\n            combined_content: \"Test content\",\n
      \           metadata: {}\n          )\n        )\n\n        allow(@mock_prompt_combiner).to
      receive(:save_prompt).and_return({\n          success: false,\n          error:
      \"No space left on device\"\n        })\n      end\n\n      it \"handles disk
      space errors during prompt saving\" do\n        expect do\n          prompt_builder.build_review_prompt(session,
      target, context)\n        end.to raise_error(\"Failed to save prompt: No space
      left on device\")\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"integration scenarios\",
      :integration_scenarios do\n    let(:complex_session) do\n      CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewSession.new(\n
      \       session_id: \"complex-integration-session\",\n        session_name:
      \"complex\",\n        timestamp: Time.now.iso8601,\n        directory_path:
      session_dir,\n        focus: \"multi-focus: architecture, security, performance\",\n
      \       target: \"src/\",\n        context_mode: \"comprehensive\",\n        metadata:
      {\n          reviewer: \"expert\",\n          priority: \"high\",\n          custom_rules:
      [\"rule1\", \"rule2\"]\n        }\n      )\n    end\n\n    let(:complex_target)
      do\n      CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewTarget.new(\n        type: \"git_diff_with_context\",\n
      \       target_spec: \"HEAD~3..HEAD\",\n        resolved_paths: [\"src/app.rb\",
      \"src/config.rb\", \"test/app_test.rb\"],\n        content_type: \"diff\",\n
      \       size_info: {files: 3, lines: 250, additions: 150, deletions: 100}\n
      \     )\n    end\n\n    let(:comprehensive_context) do\n      CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewContext.new(\n
      \       mode: \"comprehensive\",\n        documents: [\n          {type: \"blueprint\",
      path: \"README.md\", content: \"# Complex Project Blueprint\"},\n          {type:
      \"vision\", path: \"docs/vision.md\", content: \"# Project Vision\"},\n          {type:
      \"architecture\", path: \"docs/architecture.md\", content: \"# System Architecture\"},\n
      \         {type: \"security\", path: \"docs/security.md\", content: \"# Security
      Guidelines\"},\n          {type: \"performance\", path: \"docs/performance.md\",
      content: \"# Performance Standards\"}\n        ],\n        loaded_at: Time.now\n
      \     )\n    end\n\n    let(:complex_prompt) do\n      CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewPrompt.new(\n
      \       session_id: \"complex-integration-session\",\n        focus_areas: [\n
      \         \"Architecture compliance (see docs/architecture.md)\",\n          \"Security
      review (see docs/security.md)\",\n          \"Performance analysis (see docs/performance.md)\"\n
      \       ],\n        system_prompt_path: \"prompts/multi-focus.md\",\n        combined_content:
      \"---\\nfocus: architecture security performance\\nsession: complex-integration\\n---\\nComplex
      multi-focus review content with comprehensive context\",\n        metadata:
      {\n          generated_at: Time.now.iso8601,\n          complexity: \"high\"\n
      \       }\n      )\n    end\n\n    before do\n      # Create complex diff content\n
      \     complex_diff = <<~DIFF\n        diff --git a/src/app.rb b/src/app.rb\n
      \       index 1234567..abcdefg 100644\n        --- a/src/app.rb\n        +++
      b/src/app.rb\n        @@ -1,10 +1,15 @@\n         class App\n        +  include
      SecurityMixin\n        +  \n           def initialize\n             @config
      = load_config\n        +    @monitor = PerformanceMonitor.new\n           end\n
      \          \n           def process_request(request)\n        +    @monitor.start_timer\n
      \            result = handle_request(request)\n        +    @monitor.end_timer\n
      \            result\n           end\n         end\n      DIFF\n\n      File.write(File.join(session_dir,
      \"input.diff\"), complex_diff)\n\n      allow(@mock_file_reader).to receive(:read).with(File.join(session_dir,
      \"input.diff\")).and_return({\n        success: true,\n        content: complex_diff\n
      \     })\n\n      allow(@mock_prompt_combiner).to receive(:build_prompt).and_return(complex_prompt)\n
      \     allow(@mock_prompt_combiner).to receive(:save_prompt).and_return({success:
      true, error: nil})\n    end\n\n    it \"handles complex multi-focus integration
      scenario\" do\n      result = prompt_builder.build_review_prompt(complex_session,
      complex_target, comprehensive_context)\n\n      expect(@mock_prompt_combiner).to
      have_received(:build_prompt).with(\n        complex_session,\n        kind_of(String),\n
      \       comprehensive_context,\n        \"multi-focus: architecture, security,
      performance\",\n        nil\n      )\n\n      expect(result).to eq(complex_prompt)\n
      \     expect(result.focus_areas.size).to eq(3)\n      expect(result.combined_content).to
      include(\"multi-focus\")\n    end\n\n    it \"processes large diffs efficiently\"
      do\n      large_diff = \"diff content\\n\" * 1000  # Simulate large diff\n      File.write(File.join(session_dir,
      \"input.diff\"), large_diff)\n\n      allow(@mock_file_reader).to receive(:read).with(File.join(session_dir,
      \"input.diff\")).and_return({\n        success: true,\n        content: large_diff\n
      \     })\n\n      prompt_builder.build_review_prompt(complex_session, complex_target,
      comprehensive_context)\n\n      expect(@mock_prompt_combiner).to have_received(:build_prompt).with(\n
      \       complex_session,\n        large_diff,\n        comprehensive_context,\n
      \       \"multi-focus: architecture, security, performance\",\n        nil\n
      \     )\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"boundary conditions\", :boundary_conditions
      do\n    context \"temporary session creation edge cases\" do\n      let(:minimal_context)
      do\n        CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewContext.new(\n          mode:
      \"minimal\",\n          documents: [],\n          loaded_at: Time.now\n        )\n
      \     end\n\n      let(:mock_immediate_prompt) do\n        CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewPrompt.new(\n
      \         session_id: \"temp-123\",\n          focus_areas: [\"Security\"],\n
      \         system_prompt_path: \"prompts/security.md\",\n          combined_content:
      \"Immediate boundary test content\",\n          metadata: {generated_at: Time.now.iso8601}\n
      \       )\n      end\n\n      before do\n        allow(@mock_prompt_combiner).to
      receive(:build_prompt).and_return(mock_immediate_prompt)\n        allow(Dir).to
      receive(:tmpdir).and_return(\"/tmp\")\n      end\n\n      it \"handles empty
      target content\" do\n        result = prompt_builder.build_immediate_prompt(\"security\",
      \"\", minimal_context)\n\n        expect(@mock_prompt_combiner).to have_received(:build_prompt)
      do |session, content, _ctx, _focus|\n          expect(content).to eq(\"\")\n
      \         expect(session.session_id).to start_with(\"temp-\")\n          expect(session.focus).to
      eq(\"security\")\n        end\n\n        expect(result).to eq(\"Immediate boundary
      test content\")\n      end\n\n      it \"handles very long target content\"
      do\n        long_content = \"x\" * 100_000  # 100KB of content\n\n        result
      = prompt_builder.build_immediate_prompt(\"architecture\", long_content, minimal_context)\n\n
      \       expect(@mock_prompt_combiner).to have_received(:build_prompt) do |session,
      content, _ctx, _focus|\n          expect(content.length).to eq(100_000)\n          expect(session.session_id).to
      start_with(\"temp-\")\n        end\n\n        expect(result).to eq(\"Immediate
      boundary test content\")\n      end\n\n      it \"handles special characters
      in focus\" do\n        special_focus = \"security & performance (high-priority!)\"\n\n
      \       result = prompt_builder.build_immediate_prompt(special_focus, \"content\",
      minimal_context)\n\n        expect(@mock_prompt_combiner).to have_received(:build_prompt)
      do |session, _content, _ctx, focus|\n          expect(session.focus).to eq(special_focus)\n
      \         expect(focus).to eq(special_focus)\n        end\n\n        expect(result).to
      eq(\"Immediate boundary test content\")\n      end\n\n      it \"creates temporary
      sessions with correct attributes\" do\n        # Test session creation mechanism
      rather than uniqueness (which depends on timing)\n        captured_session =
      nil\n        allow(@mock_prompt_combiner).to receive(:build_prompt) do |session,
      *_args|\n          captured_session = session\n          mock_immediate_prompt\n
      \       end\n\n        result = prompt_builder.build_immediate_prompt(\"security\",
      \"test content\", minimal_context)\n\n        expect(@mock_prompt_combiner).to
      have_received(:build_prompt).once\n        expect(captured_session).not_to be_nil\n\n
      \       # Verify session attributes\n        expect(captured_session.session_id).to
      start_with(\"temp-\")\n        expect(captured_session.session_name).to eq(\"temp\")\n
      \       expect(captured_session.directory_path).to eq(\"/tmp\")\n        expect(captured_session.focus).to
      eq(\"security\")\n        expect(captured_session.target).to eq(\"immediate\")\n
      \       expect(captured_session.context_mode).to eq(\"minimal\")\n        expect(captured_session.metadata).to
      eq({})\n\n        expect(result).to eq(\"Immediate boundary test content\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"statistics edge cases\", :statistics_edge_cases
      do\n    context \"with malformed prompt data\" do\n      let(:malformed_prompt)
      do\n        CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewPrompt.new(\n          session_id:
      \"\",\n          focus_areas: [],\n          system_prompt_path: \"\",\n          combined_content:
      \"\",\n          metadata: {}\n        )\n      end\n\n      it \"handles empty
      prompt gracefully\" do\n        stats = prompt_builder.get_prompt_stats(malformed_prompt)\n\n
      \       expect(stats[:size_bytes]).to eq(0)\n        expect(stats[:word_count]).to
      eq(0)\n        expect(stats[:multi_focus]).to be false\n        expect(stats[:focus_count]).to
      eq(0)\n        expect(stats[:has_frontmatter]).to be false\n        expect(stats[:session_id]).to
      eq(\"\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with null/nil prompt fields\"
      do\n      let(:null_fields_prompt) do\n        # Create a prompt with potential
      nil fields\n        prompt = CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewPrompt.new(\n
      \         session_id: \"test-nil-session\",\n          focus_areas: [\"Architecture\"],\n
      \         system_prompt_path: \"prompts/test.md\",\n          combined_content:
      \"Test content\",\n          metadata: {}\n        )\n\n        # Simulate nil
      fields that might occur in edge cases\n        allow(prompt).to receive(:primary_focus).and_return(nil)\n
      \       prompt\n      end\n\n      it \"handles nil primary focus\" do\n        stats
      = prompt_builder.get_prompt_stats(null_fields_prompt)\n\n        expect(stats[:primary_focus]).to
      be_nil\n        expect(stats[:focus_count]).to eq(1)\n        expect(stats[:session_id]).to
      eq(\"test-nil-session\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with extremely
      large prompts\" do\n      let(:large_prompt) do\n        large_content = \"word
      \" * 50_000  # 50k words\n        CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewPrompt.new(\n
      \         session_id: \"large-session\",\n          focus_areas: [\"Architecture\"]
      * 100,  # 100 focus areas\n          system_prompt_path: \"prompts/large.md\",\n
      \         combined_content: large_content,\n          metadata: {large: true}\n
      \       )\n      end\n\n      it \"handles very large prompts efficiently\"
      do\n        stats = prompt_builder.get_prompt_stats(large_prompt)\n\n        expect(stats[:word_count]).to
      eq(50_000)\n        expect(stats[:focus_count]).to eq(100)\n        expect(stats[:size_bytes]).to
      be > 200_000  # At least 200KB\n        expect(stats[:session_id]).to eq(\"large-session\")\n
      \     end\n    end\n  end\nend\n"
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/organisms/code/review_manager_spec.rb
    size: 20061
    content: "# frozen_string_literal: true\n\nrequire \"spec_helper\"\nrequire \"tmpdir\"\nrequire
      \"fileutils\"\nrequire \"coding_agent_tools/organisms/code/review_manager\"\n\nRSpec.describe
      CodingAgentTools::Organisms::Code::ReviewManager do\n  let(:review_manager)
      { described_class.new }\n  let(:temp_dir) { Dir.mktmpdir(\"review_manager_test\")
      }\n  let(:session_dir) { File.join(temp_dir, \"session\") }\n\n  # Mock organisms\n
      \ let(:mock_session_manager) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::Code::SessionManager)
      }\n  let(:mock_content_extractor) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::Code::ContentExtractor)
      }\n  let(:mock_context_loader) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::Code::ContextLoader)
      }\n  let(:mock_prompt_builder) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::Code::PromptBuilder)
      }\n\n  # Mock model objects\n  let(:mock_session) do\n    double(\"ReviewSession\",\n
      \     session_id: \"20240101-120000\",\n      session_name: \"review-20240101-120000\",\n
      \     timestamp: \"2024-01-01T12:00:00Z\",\n      directory_path: session_dir,\n
      \     focus: \"code\",\n      target: \"HEAD~1..HEAD\",\n      context_mode_with_default:
      \"auto\")\n  end\n\n  let(:mock_target) do\n    double(\"ReviewTarget\",\n      type:
      \"git_diff\",\n      content_type: \"diff\",\n      file_count: 3,\n      line_count:
      150,\n      size_info: {files: 3, lines: 150})\n  end\n\n  let(:mock_context)
      do\n    double(\"ReviewContext\",\n      mode: \"auto\",\n      document_count:
      2)\n  end\n\n  let(:mock_prompt) do\n    double(\"ReviewPrompt\",\n      word_count:
      500,\n      focus_areas: [\"code\"],\n      system_prompt_path: \"review/code-review.md\")\n
      \ end\n\n  before do\n    # Mock organism instantiation\n    allow(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::Code::SessionManager).to
      receive(:new).and_return(mock_session_manager)\n    allow(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::Code::ContentExtractor).to
      receive(:new).and_return(mock_content_extractor)\n    allow(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::Code::ContextLoader).to
      receive(:new).and_return(mock_context_loader)\n    allow(CodingAgentTools::Organisms::Code::PromptBuilder).to
      receive(:new).and_return(mock_prompt_builder)\n\n    # Create session directory
      for file operations\n    FileUtils.mkdir_p(session_dir)\n  end\n\n  after do\n
      \   safe_directory_cleanup(temp_dir)\n  end\n\n  describe \"#initialize\" do\n
      \   it \"initializes all required organisms\" do\n      expect(review_manager.session_manager).to
      eq(mock_session_manager)\n      expect(review_manager.content_extractor).to
      eq(mock_content_extractor)\n      expect(review_manager.context_loader).to eq(mock_context_loader)\n
      \     expect(review_manager.prompt_builder).to eq(mock_prompt_builder)\n    end\n
      \ end\n\n  describe \"#create_review_session\" do\n    let(:focus) { \"code\"
      }\n    let(:target) { \"HEAD~1..HEAD\" }\n    let(:context) { \"auto\" }\n    let(:base_path)
      { nil }\n    let(:system_prompt_override) { nil }\n\n    context \"when all
      operations succeed\" do\n      before do\n        # Setup successful organism
      responses\n        allow(mock_session_manager).to receive(:create_session).and_return(mock_session)\n
      \       allow(mock_content_extractor).to receive(:extract_and_save).and_return(mock_target)\n
      \       allow(mock_context_loader).to receive(:load_context).and_return(mock_context)\n
      \       allow(mock_context_loader).to receive(:save_context)\n        allow(mock_context_loader).to
      receive(:get_context_summary).and_return(\"Context summary\")\n        allow(mock_prompt_builder).to
      receive(:build_review_prompt).and_return(mock_prompt)\n\n        # Mock File.write
      for session summary\n        allow(File).to receive(:write)\n      end\n\n      it
      \"creates a review session successfully\" do\n        result = review_manager.create_review_session(focus,
      target, context, base_path, system_prompt_override)\n\n        expect(result[:success]).to
      be true\n        expect(result[:error]).to be nil\n        expect(result[:session]).to
      eq(mock_session)\n        expect(result[:target]).to eq(mock_target)\n        expect(result[:context]).to
      eq(mock_context)\n        expect(result[:prompt]).to eq(mock_prompt)\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"calls session manager with correct parameters\" do\n        review_manager.create_review_session(focus,
      target, context, base_path, system_prompt_override)\n\n        expect(mock_session_manager).to
      have_received(:create_session).with(\n          focus: focus,\n          target:
      target,\n          context_mode: context,\n          base_path: base_path\n
      \       )\n      end\n\n      it \"extracts and saves content\" do\n        review_manager.create_review_session(focus,
      target, context, base_path, system_prompt_override)\n\n        expect(mock_content_extractor).to
      have_received(:extract_and_save).with(target, session_dir)\n      end\n\n      it
      \"loads and saves context\" do\n        review_manager.create_review_session(focus,
      target, context, base_path, system_prompt_override)\n\n        expect(mock_context_loader).to
      have_received(:load_context).with(context, mock_session)\n        expect(mock_context_loader).to
      have_received(:save_context).with(mock_context, session_dir)\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"builds review prompt\" do\n        review_manager.create_review_session(focus,
      target, context, base_path, system_prompt_override)\n\n        expect(mock_prompt_builder).to
      have_received(:build_review_prompt).with(\n          mock_session, mock_target,
      mock_context, system_prompt_override\n        )\n      end\n\n      it \"writes
      session summary\" do\n        expected_summary = include(\"# Code Review Session
      Summary\")\n\n        review_manager.create_review_session(focus, target, context,
      base_path, system_prompt_override)\n\n        expect(File).to have_received(:write).with(\n
      \         File.join(session_dir, \"session-summary.md\"),\n          expected_summary\n
      \       )\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"when content extraction fails\"
      do\n      let(:error_target) do\n        CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewTarget.new(\n
      \         type: \"error\",\n          target_spec: \"HEAD~1..HEAD\",\n          resolved_paths:
      [],\n          content_type: \"none\",\n          size_info: {error: \"Git command
      failed\"}\n        )\n      end\n\n      before do\n        allow(mock_session_manager).to
      receive(:create_session).and_return(mock_session)\n        allow(mock_content_extractor).to
      receive(:extract_and_save).and_return(error_target)\n      end\n\n      it \"returns
      error result for failed content extraction\" do\n        result = review_manager.create_review_session(focus,
      target, context, base_path, system_prompt_override)\n\n        expect(result[:success]).to
      be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to eq(\"Failed to extract target content:
      Git command failed\")\n        expect(result[:session]).to be nil\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"when an exception occurs\" do\n      before do\n        allow(mock_session_manager).to
      receive(:create_session).and_raise(StandardError, \"Unexpected error\")\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"returns error result\" do\n        result = review_manager.create_review_session(focus,
      target, context, base_path, system_prompt_override)\n\n        expect(result[:success]).to
      be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to eq(\"Unexpected error\")\n        expect(result[:session]).to
      be nil\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with custom system prompt\" do\n
      \     let(:system_prompt_override) { \"/path/to/custom-prompt.md\" }\n\n      before
      do\n        allow(mock_session_manager).to receive(:create_session).and_return(mock_session)\n
      \       allow(mock_content_extractor).to receive(:extract_and_save).and_return(mock_target)\n
      \       allow(mock_context_loader).to receive(:load_context).and_return(mock_context)\n
      \       allow(mock_context_loader).to receive(:save_context)\n        allow(mock_context_loader).to
      receive(:get_context_summary).and_return(\"Context summary\")\n        allow(mock_prompt_builder).to
      receive(:build_review_prompt).and_return(mock_prompt)\n        allow(File).to
      receive(:write)\n      end\n\n      it \"passes system prompt override to prompt
      builder\" do\n        review_manager.create_review_session(focus, target, context,
      base_path, system_prompt_override)\n\n        expect(mock_prompt_builder).to
      have_received(:build_review_prompt).with(\n          mock_session, mock_target,
      mock_context, system_prompt_override\n        )\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n
      \ describe \"#execute_review\" do\n    it \"returns placeholder implementation
      response\" do\n      result = review_manager.execute_review(mock_session)\n\n
      \     expect(result[:success]).to be false\n      expect(result[:error]).to
      eq(\"LLM integration not yet implemented\")\n      expect(result[:reports]).to
      eq([])\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe \"#finalize_session\" do\n    let(:reports)
      do\n      [\n        {name: \"review-1\", file: \"review-1.md\", model: \"google:gemini-2.5-pro\",
      status: \"completed\"},\n        {name: \"review-2\", file: \"review-2.md\",
      model: \"anthropic:claude-3-5-sonnet\", status: \"completed\"}\n      ]\n    end\n\n
      \   before do\n      # Mock file operations\n      allow(File).to receive(:exist?).and_return(false)\n
      \     allow(File).to receive(:read).and_return(\"\")\n      allow(File).to receive(:write)\n
      \     allow(Dir).to receive(:glob).and_return([\"session.meta\", \"input.xml\",
      \"prompt.md\"])\n    end\n\n    context \"when finalization succeeds\" do\n
      \     it \"returns success\" do\n        result = review_manager.finalize_session(mock_session,
      reports)\n\n        expect(result[:success]).to be true\n        expect(result[:error]).to
      be nil\n      end\n\n      it \"updates session index with reports\" do\n        include(\"##
      Review Reports\")\n        include(\"- [`review-1`](./review-1.md) - google:gemini-2.5-pro\")\n
      \       expected_content = include(\"- [`review-2`](./review-2.md) - anthropic:claude-3-5-sonnet\")\n\n
      \       review_manager.finalize_session(mock_session, reports)\n\n        expect(File).to
      have_received(:write).with(\n          File.join(session_dir, \"README.md\"),\n
      \         expected_content\n        )\n      end\n\n      it \"writes execution
      summary\" do\n        include(\"Session: review-20240101-120000\")\n        include(\"Target:
      HEAD~1..HEAD\")\n        include(\"Focus: code\")\n        include(\"- google:gemini-2.5-pro:
      completed\")\n        expected_summary = include(\"- anthropic:claude-3-5-sonnet:
      completed\")\n\n        review_manager.finalize_session(mock_session, reports)\n\n
      \       expect(File).to have_received(:write).with(\n          File.join(session_dir,
      \"execution.summary\"),\n          expected_summary\n        )\n      end\n
      \   end\n\n    context \"when an exception occurs\" do\n      before do\n        allow(File).to
      receive(:write).and_raise(StandardError, \"Write failed\")\n      end\n\n      it
      \"returns error result\" do\n        result = review_manager.finalize_session(mock_session,
      reports)\n\n        expect(result[:success]).to be false\n        expect(result[:error]).to
      eq(\"Write failed\")\n      end\n    end\n\n    context \"with existing README
      content\" do\n      let(:existing_readme) do\n        <<~README\n          #
      Existing Session\n          \n          Some existing content.\n          \n
      \         ## Review Reports\n          \n          - Old report\n          \n
      \         ## Other Section\n          \n          Other content.\n        README\n
      \     end\n\n      before do\n        allow(File).to receive(:exist?).with(File.join(session_dir,
      \"README.md\")).and_return(true)\n        allow(File).to receive(:read).with(File.join(session_dir,
      \"README.md\")).and_return(existing_readme)\n      end\n\n      it \"replaces
      existing reports section\" do\n        include(\"## Review Reports\")\n        include(\"-
      [`review-1`](./review-1.md) - google:gemini-2.5-pro\")\n        include(\"##
      Other Section\")\n        expected_content = include(\"Other content.\")\n\n
      \       review_manager.finalize_session(mock_session, reports)\n\n        expect(File).to
      have_received(:write).with(\n          File.join(session_dir, \"README.md\"),\n
      \         expected_content\n        )\n      end\n    end\n  end\n\n  describe
      \"#prepare_review\" do\n    let(:focus) { \"code tests\" }\n    let(:target)
      { \"HEAD~1..HEAD\" }\n    let(:context) { \"auto\" }\n    let(:system_prompt_override)
      { \"/custom/prompt.md\" }\n\n    let(:target_info) { {type: \"git_diff\", format:
      \"diff\"} }\n    let(:context_info) do\n      {\n        available: true,\n
      \       found: [\n          {type: \"README\", path: \"README.md\"},\n          {type:
      \"Architecture\", path: \"docs/architecture.md\"}\n        ]\n      }\n    end\n
      \   let(:system_prompt) { \"review/code-review.md\" }\n    let(:focus_areas)
      { [\"code review\", \"test analysis\"] }\n\n    before do\n      # Mock diff
      extractor for analyze_target method\n      mock_diff_extractor = instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Code::GitDiffExtractor)\n
      \     allow(mock_content_extractor).to receive(:instance_variable_get).with(:@diff_extractor).and_return(mock_diff_extractor)\n
      \     allow(mock_diff_extractor).to receive(:git_diff_target?).with(target).and_return(true)\n\n
      \     allow(mock_context_loader).to receive(:check_availability).and_return(context_info)\n
      \     allow(mock_prompt_builder).to receive(:select_system_prompt).and_return(system_prompt)\n
      \     allow(CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewPrompt).to receive(:get_focus_descriptions).with(\"code\").and_return([\"code
      review\"])\n      allow(CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewPrompt).to receive(:get_focus_descriptions).with(\"tests\").and_return([\"test
      analysis\"])\n    end\n\n    it \"returns preparation results\" do\n      result
      = review_manager.prepare_review(focus, target, context, system_prompt_override)\n\n
      \     expect(result[:target_info]).to eq(target_info)\n      expect(result[:context_info]).to
      eq(context_info)\n      expect(result[:system_prompt]).to eq(system_prompt)\n
      \     expect(result[:focus_areas]).to eq(focus_areas)\n    end\n\n    it \"checks
      context availability\" do\n      review_manager.prepare_review(focus, target,
      context, system_prompt_override)\n\n      expect(mock_context_loader).to have_received(:check_availability)\n
      \   end\n\n    it \"selects system prompt\" do\n      review_manager.prepare_review(focus,
      target, context, system_prompt_override)\n\n      expect(mock_prompt_builder).to
      have_received(:select_system_prompt).with(focus, system_prompt_override)\n    end\n\n
      \   it \"analyzes target\" do\n      # Mock the diff extractor access\n      mock_diff_extractor
      = instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Code::GitDiffExtractor)\n      allow(mock_content_extractor).to
      receive(:instance_variable_get).with(:@diff_extractor).and_return(mock_diff_extractor)\n
      \     allow(mock_diff_extractor).to receive(:git_diff_target?).with(target).and_return(true)\n\n
      \     result = review_manager.prepare_review(focus, target, context, system_prompt_override)\n\n
      \     expect(result[:target_info]).to eq({type: \"git_diff\", format: \"diff\"})\n
      \   end\n  end\n\n  describe \"private methods\" do\n    describe \"#analyze_target\"
      do\n      context \"when target is a git diff\" do\n        let(:target) { \"HEAD~1..HEAD\"
      }\n\n        before do\n          mock_diff_extractor = instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Code::GitDiffExtractor)\n
      \         allow(mock_content_extractor).to receive(:instance_variable_get).with(:@diff_extractor).and_return(mock_diff_extractor)\n
      \         allow(mock_diff_extractor).to receive(:git_diff_target?).with(target).and_return(true)\n
      \       end\n\n        it \"returns git_diff type\" do\n          result = review_manager.send(:analyze_target,
      target)\n\n          expect(result[:type]).to eq(\"git_diff\")\n          expect(result[:format]).to
      eq(\"diff\")\n        end\n      end\n\n      context \"when target is a single
      file\" do\n        let(:target) { \"/path/to/file.rb\" }\n\n        before do\n
      \         mock_diff_extractor = instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Code::GitDiffExtractor)\n
      \         allow(mock_content_extractor).to receive(:instance_variable_get).with(:@diff_extractor).and_return(mock_diff_extractor)\n
      \         allow(mock_diff_extractor).to receive(:git_diff_target?).with(target).and_return(false)\n
      \         allow(File).to receive(:exist?).and_call_original\n          allow(File).to
      receive(:exist?).with(target).and_return(true)\n          allow(File).to receive(:directory?).with(target).and_return(false)\n
      \       end\n\n        it \"returns single_file type\" do\n          result
      = review_manager.send(:analyze_target, target)\n\n          expect(result[:type]).to
      eq(\"single_file\")\n          expect(result[:format]).to eq(\"xml\")\n          expect(result[:path]).to
      eq(target)\n        end\n      end\n\n      context \"when target is a file
      pattern\" do\n        let(:target) { \"*.rb\" }\n\n        before do\n          mock_diff_extractor
      = instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Code::GitDiffExtractor)\n          allow(mock_content_extractor).to
      receive(:instance_variable_get).with(:@diff_extractor).and_return(mock_diff_extractor)\n
      \         allow(mock_diff_extractor).to receive(:git_diff_target?).with(target).and_return(false)\n
      \         allow(File).to receive(:exist?).and_call_original\n          allow(File).to
      receive(:exist?).with(target).and_return(false)\n        end\n\n        it \"returns
      file_pattern type\" do\n          result = review_manager.send(:analyze_target,
      target)\n\n          expect(result[:type]).to eq(\"file_pattern\")\n          expect(result[:format]).to
      eq(\"xml\")\n          expect(result[:pattern]).to eq(target)\n        end\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    describe \"#write_session_summary\" do\n      before
      do\n        allow(File).to receive(:write)\n        allow(mock_context_loader).to
      receive(:get_context_summary).and_return(\"Context summary text\")\n      end\n\n
      \     it \"writes comprehensive session summary\" do\n        review_manager.send(:write_session_summary,
      mock_session, mock_target, mock_context, mock_prompt)\n\n        expected_path
      = File.join(session_dir, \"session-summary.md\")\n        include(\"# Code Review
      Session Summary\")\n        include(\"**ID**: 20240101-120000\")\n        include(\"**Name**:
      review-20240101-120000\")\n        include(\"**Focus**: code\")\n        include(\"**Target**:
      HEAD~1..HEAD\")\n        include(\"**Type**: git_diff\")\n        include(\"**Files**:
      3\")\n        include(\"**Lines**: 150\")\n        include(\"**Size**: 500 words\")\n
      \       expected_content = include(\"Context summary text\")\n\n        expect(File).to
      have_received(:write).with(expected_path, expected_content)\n      end\n    end\n\n
      \   describe \"#update_session_index\" do\n      let(:reports) do\n        [\n
      \         {name: \"review-1\", file: \"review-1.md\", model: \"google:gemini-2.5-pro\"},\n
      \         {name: \"review-2\", file: \"review-2.md\", model: \"anthropic:claude-3-5-sonnet\"}\n
      \       ]\n      end\n\n      before do\n        allow(File).to receive(:exist?).and_return(false)\n
      \       allow(File).to receive(:read).and_return(\"\")\n        allow(File).to
      receive(:write)\n      end\n\n      it \"creates new index with reports section\"
      do\n        review_manager.send(:update_session_index, mock_session, reports)\n\n
      \       expected_path = File.join(session_dir, \"README.md\")\n        include(\"##
      Review Reports\")\n        include(\"- [`review-1`](./review-1.md) - google:gemini-2.5-pro\")\n
      \       expected_content = include(\"- [`review-2`](./review-2.md) - anthropic:claude-3-5-sonnet\")\n\n
      \       expect(File).to have_received(:write).with(expected_path, expected_content)\n
      \     end\n    end\n\n    describe \"#write_execution_summary\" do\n      let(:reports)
      do\n        [\n          {model: \"google:gemini-2.5-pro\", status: \"completed\"},\n
      \         {model: \"anthropic:claude-3-5-sonnet\", status: \"failed\"}\n        ]\n
      \     end\n\n      before do\n        allow(File).to receive(:write)\n        allow(Dir).to
      receive(:glob).and_return([\"session.meta\", \"input.xml\", \"prompt.md\"])\n
      \       allow(Time).to receive(:now).and_return(Time.parse(\"2024-01-01T15:30:00Z\"))\n
      \     end\n\n      it \"writes execution summary with results\" do\n        review_manager.send(:write_execution_summary,
      mock_session, reports)\n\n        expected_path = File.join(session_dir, \"execution.summary\")\n
      \       include(\"Session: review-20240101-120000\")\n        include(\"Target:
      HEAD~1..HEAD\")\n        include(\"Focus: code\")\n        include(\"- google:gemini-2.5-pro:
      completed\")\n        include(\"- anthropic:claude-3-5-sonnet: failed\")\n        include(\"session.meta\")\n
      \       include(\"input.xml\")\n        expected_content = include(\"prompt.md\")\n\n
      \       expect(File).to have_received(:write).with(expected_path, expected_content)\n
      \     end\n    end\n  end\nend\n"
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/organisms/code/session_manager_spec.rb
    size: 25110
    content: |
      # frozen_string_literal: true

      require "spec_helper"
      require "tmpdir"
      require "fileutils"
      require "time"
      require "coding_agent_tools/organisms/code/session_manager"

      RSpec.describe CodingAgentTools::Organisms::Code::SessionManager do
        let(:session_manager) { described_class.new }
        let(:temp_dir) { Dir.mktmpdir("session_manager_test") }
        let(:base_path) { File.join(temp_dir, "sessions") }

        # Mock dependencies
        let(:mock_session_builder) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Code::SessionDirectoryBuilder) }
        let(:mock_file_handler) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::FileIoHandler) }
        let(:mock_file_reader) { instance_double(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::Code::FileContentReader) }

        # Sample session data
        let(:sample_session) do
          CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewSession.new(
            session_id: "review-20240101-120000",
            session_name: "code-HEAD~1..HEAD-20240101-120000",
            timestamp: "2024-01-01T12:00:00Z",
            directory_path: File.join(base_path, "code-HEAD~1..HEAD-20240101-120000"),
            focus: "code",
            target: "HEAD~1..HEAD",
            context_mode: "auto",
            metadata: {created_at: Time.parse("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z")}
          )
        end

        let(:session_params) do
          {
            focus: "code",
            target: "HEAD~1..HEAD",
            context_mode: "auto",
            base_path: base_path
          }
        end

        before do
          # Mock dependency instantiation
          allow(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Code::SessionDirectoryBuilder).to receive(:new).and_return(mock_session_builder)
          allow(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::FileIoHandler).to receive(:new).and_return(mock_file_handler)
          allow(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::Code::FileContentReader).to receive(:new).and_return(mock_file_reader)

          # Create base directory for tests
          FileUtils.mkdir_p(base_path)
        end

        after do
          safe_directory_cleanup(temp_dir)
        end

        describe "#initialize" do
          it "initializes all required dependencies" do
            # Create a new instance to trigger the initialization
            described_class.new

            expect(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::Code::SessionDirectoryBuilder).to have_received(:new)
            expect(CodingAgentTools::Molecules::FileIoHandler).to have_received(:new)
            expect(CodingAgentTools::Atoms::Code::FileContentReader).to have_received(:new)
          end
        end

        describe "#create_session" do
          context "when all required parameters are provided" do
            before do
              allow(mock_session_builder).to receive(:build_full_session).and_return(sample_session)
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:create_session_files)
            end

            it "creates a session successfully" do
              result = session_manager.create_session(session_params)

              expect(result).to eq(sample_session)
              expect(mock_session_builder).to have_received(:build_full_session).with(
                "code",
                "HEAD~1..HEAD",
                "auto",
                base_path
              )
            end

            it "creates additional session files" do
              expect(session_manager).to receive(:create_session_files).with(sample_session)

              session_manager.create_session(session_params)
            end
          end

          context "when required parameters are missing" do
            it "raises error when focus is missing" do
              params = session_params.dup
              params.delete(:focus)

              expect { session_manager.create_session(params) }.to raise_error(ArgumentError, "focus is required")
            end

            it "raises error when target is missing" do
              params = session_params.dup
              params.delete(:target)

              expect { session_manager.create_session(params) }.to raise_error(ArgumentError, "target is required")
            end

            it "raises error when focus is nil" do
              params = session_params.merge(focus: nil)

              expect { session_manager.create_session(params) }.to raise_error(ArgumentError, "focus is required")
            end

            it "raises error when target is nil" do
              params = session_params.merge(target: nil)

              expect { session_manager.create_session(params) }.to raise_error(ArgumentError, "target is required")
            end
          end

          context "when optional parameters have defaults" do
            let(:minimal_params) { {focus: "code", target: "HEAD~1..HEAD"} }

            before do
              allow(mock_session_builder).to receive(:build_full_session).and_return(sample_session)
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:create_session_files)
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:default_base_path).and_return("/default/path")
            end

            it "uses default context_mode when not provided" do
              session_manager.create_session(minimal_params)

              expect(mock_session_builder).to have_received(:build_full_session).with(
                "code",
                "HEAD~1..HEAD",
                "auto",
                "/default/path"
              )
            end

            it "uses default base_path when not provided" do
              expect(session_manager).to receive(:default_base_path).and_return("/default/path")

              session_manager.create_session(minimal_params)

              expect(mock_session_builder).to have_received(:build_full_session).with(
                "code",
                "HEAD~1..HEAD",
                "auto",
                "/default/path"
              )
            end
          end

          context "when context_mode is explicitly provided" do
            let(:custom_params) { session_params.merge(context_mode: "none") }

            before do
              allow(mock_session_builder).to receive(:build_full_session).and_return(sample_session)
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:create_session_files)
            end

            it "uses the provided context_mode" do
              session_manager.create_session(custom_params)

              expect(mock_session_builder).to have_received(:build_full_session).with(
                "code",
                "HEAD~1..HEAD",
                "none",
                base_path
              )
            end
          end
        end

        describe "#load_session" do
          let(:session_id) { "review-20240101-120000" }
          let(:session_dir) { File.join(base_path, "code-HEAD~1..HEAD-20240101-120000") }
          let(:metadata_path) { File.join(session_dir, "session.meta") }
          let(:metadata_content) do
            <<~METADATA
              command: @review-code code HEAD~1..HEAD auto
              timestamp: 2024-01-01T12:00:00Z
              target: HEAD~1..HEAD
              focus: code
              context: auto
            METADATA
          end

          context "when session exists and is valid" do
            before do
              FileUtils.mkdir_p(session_dir)
              File.write(metadata_path, metadata_content)
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:find_session_directory).and_return(session_dir)
            end

            it "loads session successfully" do
              result = session_manager.load_session(session_id, base_path)

              expect(result).to be_a(CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewSession)
              expect(result.session_id).to eq(session_id)
              expect(result.session_name).to eq("code-HEAD~1..HEAD-20240101-120000")
              expect(result.timestamp).to eq("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z")
              expect(result.directory_path).to eq(session_dir)
              expect(result.focus).to eq("code")
              expect(result.target).to eq("HEAD~1..HEAD")
              expect(result.context_mode).to eq("auto")
            end

            it "parses metadata correctly" do
              result = session_manager.load_session(session_id, base_path)

              expect(result.metadata[:timestamp]).to eq("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z")
              expect(result.metadata[:focus]).to eq("code")
              expect(result.metadata[:target]).to eq("HEAD~1..HEAD")
              expect(result.metadata[:context]).to eq("auto")
            end
          end

          context "when session directory does not exist" do
            before do
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:find_session_directory).and_return(nil)
            end

            it "returns nil" do
              result = session_manager.load_session(session_id, base_path)

              expect(result).to be_nil
            end
          end

          context "when metadata file does not exist" do
            before do
              FileUtils.mkdir_p(session_dir)
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:find_session_directory).and_return(session_dir)
            end

            it "returns nil" do
              result = session_manager.load_session(session_id, base_path)

              expect(result).to be_nil
            end
          end

          context "when metadata is invalid" do
            let(:invalid_metadata) { "invalid metadata content" }

            before do
              FileUtils.mkdir_p(session_dir)
              File.write(metadata_path, invalid_metadata)
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:find_session_directory).and_return(session_dir)
            end

            it "returns nil for invalid metadata" do
              result = session_manager.load_session(session_id, base_path)

              expect(result).to be_nil
            end
          end

          context "when base_path is not provided" do
            before do
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:default_base_path).and_return(base_path)
              FileUtils.mkdir_p(session_dir)
              File.write(metadata_path, metadata_content)
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:find_session_directory).and_return(session_dir)
            end

            it "uses default base path" do
              expect(session_manager).to receive(:default_base_path)

              session_manager.load_session(session_id)
            end
          end
        end

        describe "#list_sessions" do
          let(:session1_dir) { File.join(base_path, "code-feature-20240101-120000") }
          let(:session2_dir) { File.join(base_path, "tests-bugfix-20240102-140000") }
          let(:session3_dir) { File.join(base_path, "docs-update-20240103-160000") }

          let(:metadata1) do
            <<~METADATA
              timestamp: 2024-01-01T12:00:00Z
              focus: code
              target: feature-branch
            METADATA
          end

          let(:metadata2) do
            <<~METADATA
              timestamp: 2024-01-02T14:00:00Z
              focus: tests
              target: bugfix-branch
            METADATA
          end

          let(:metadata3) do
            <<~METADATA
              timestamp: 2024-01-03T16:00:00Z
              focus: docs
              target: update-branch
            METADATA
          end

          before do
            # Create session directories and metadata
            [session1_dir, session2_dir, session3_dir].each { |dir| FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) }

            File.write(File.join(session1_dir, "session.meta"), metadata1)
            File.write(File.join(session2_dir, "session.meta"), metadata2)
            File.write(File.join(session3_dir, "session.meta"), metadata3)
          end

          context "when sessions exist" do
            it "returns all sessions" do
              sessions = session_manager.list_sessions(base_path)

              expect(sessions.size).to eq(3)
              expect(sessions.map { |s| s[:focus] }).to contain_exactly("code", "tests", "docs")
            end

            it "sorts sessions by timestamp descending" do
              sessions = session_manager.list_sessions(base_path)

              expect(sessions[0][:timestamp]).to eq("2024-01-03T16:00:00Z")
              expect(sessions[1][:timestamp]).to eq("2024-01-02T14:00:00Z")
              expect(sessions[2][:timestamp]).to eq("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z")
            end

            it "includes all required session information" do
              sessions = session_manager.list_sessions(base_path)
              session = sessions.first

              expect(session).to include(:session_name, :timestamp, :focus, :target, :path)
              expect(session[:session_name]).to eq("docs-update-20240103-160000")
              expect(session[:focus]).to eq("docs")
              expect(session[:target]).to eq("update-branch")
              expect(session[:path]).to eq(session3_dir)
            end
          end

          context "when no sessions exist" do
            let(:empty_base_path) { File.join(temp_dir, "empty_sessions") }

            before do
              FileUtils.mkdir_p(empty_base_path)
            end

            it "returns empty array" do
              sessions = session_manager.list_sessions(empty_base_path)

              expect(sessions).to be_empty
            end
          end

          context "when some directories lack metadata" do
            let(:invalid_session_dir) { File.join(base_path, "invalid-session-20240104-180000") }

            before do
              FileUtils.mkdir_p(invalid_session_dir)
              # No metadata file created
            end

            it "skips directories without metadata" do
              sessions = session_manager.list_sessions(base_path)

              expect(sessions.size).to eq(3)
              expect(sessions.map { |s| s[:session_name] }).not_to include("invalid-session-20240104-180000")
            end
          end

          context "when base_path is not provided" do
            before do
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:default_base_path).and_return(base_path)
            end

            it "uses default base path" do
              expect(session_manager).to receive(:default_base_path)

              session_manager.list_sessions
            end
          end
        end

        describe "#cleanup_old_sessions" do
          let(:old_session_dir) { File.join(base_path, "old-session-20240101-120000") }
          let(:recent_session_dir) { File.join(base_path, "recent-session-20240114-120000") }

          let(:old_metadata) do
            <<~METADATA
              timestamp: 2024-01-01T12:00:00Z
              focus: code
              target: old-feature
            METADATA
          end

          let(:recent_metadata) do
            <<~METADATA
              timestamp: 2024-01-14T12:00:00Z
              focus: code
              target: recent-feature
            METADATA
          end

          before do
            # Create session directories
            FileUtils.mkdir_p(old_session_dir)
            FileUtils.mkdir_p(recent_session_dir)

            # Create metadata files
            File.write(File.join(old_session_dir, "session.meta"), old_metadata)
            File.write(File.join(recent_session_dir, "session.meta"), recent_metadata)

            # Mock current time to be after both sessions
            allow(Time).to receive(:now).and_return(Time.parse("2024-01-15T12:00:00Z"))
          end

          context "when cleaning up sessions older than specified days" do
            it "removes sessions older than cutoff" do
              # Clean sessions older than 7 days (from 2024-01-15, cutoff is 2024-01-08)
              removed = session_manager.cleanup_old_sessions(7, base_path)

              expect(removed).to include(old_session_dir)
              expect(removed).not_to include(recent_session_dir)
              expect(File.exist?(old_session_dir)).to be false
              expect(File.exist?(recent_session_dir)).to be true
            end

            it "does not remove recent sessions" do
              # Clean sessions older than 3 days (from 2024-01-15, cutoff is 2024-01-12)
              # recent_session_dir has timestamp 2024-01-14, so it should NOT be removed
              removed = session_manager.cleanup_old_sessions(3, base_path)

              expect(removed).to include(old_session_dir)
              expect(removed).not_to include(recent_session_dir)
              expect(File.exist?(recent_session_dir)).to be true
            end

            it "returns list of removed session paths" do
              removed = session_manager.cleanup_old_sessions(7, base_path)

              expect(removed).to be_an(Array)
              expect(removed).to include(old_session_dir)
            end
          end

          context "when no sessions are old enough" do
            it "returns empty array" do
              # Clean sessions older than 20 days (both sessions are recent)
              removed = session_manager.cleanup_old_sessions(20, base_path)

              expect(removed).to be_empty
              expect(File.exist?(old_session_dir)).to be true
              expect(File.exist?(recent_session_dir)).to be true
            end
          end

          context "when base_path is not provided" do
            before do
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:default_base_path).and_return(base_path)
            end

            it "uses default base path" do
              expect(session_manager).to receive(:default_base_path)

              session_manager.cleanup_old_sessions(7)
            end
          end
        end

        describe "private methods" do
          describe "#default_base_path" do
            context "when current release directory exists" do
              let(:release_dir) { File.join(temp_dir, "dev-taskflow", "current", "v.0.3.0-workflows") }

              before do
                FileUtils.mkdir_p(release_dir)
                allow(File).to receive(:exist?).and_call_original
                allow(File).to receive(:exist?).with("dev-taskflow/current").and_return(true)
                allow(File).to receive(:directory?).and_call_original
                allow(File).to receive(:directory?).with("dev-taskflow/current").and_return(true)
                allow(Dir).to receive(:glob).with(File.join("dev-taskflow/current", "v.*")).and_return([release_dir])
              end

              it "returns code_review path within current release" do
                result = session_manager.send(:default_base_path)

                expect(result).to eq(File.join(release_dir, "code_review"))
              end
            end

            context "when current release directory does not exist" do
              before do
                allow(File).to receive(:exist?).and_call_original
                allow(File).to receive(:exist?).with("dev-taskflow/current").and_return(false)
              end

              it "returns system temp directory with code_review suffix" do
                result = session_manager.send(:default_base_path)

                expect(result).to eq(File.join(Dir.tmpdir, "code_review"))
              end
            end
          end

          describe "#find_session_directory" do
            let(:session_id) { "review-20240101-120000" }
            let(:matching_dir) { File.join(base_path, "code-HEAD-#{session_id}") }

            before do
              FileUtils.mkdir_p(matching_dir)
            end

            it "finds directory containing session ID" do
              result = session_manager.send(:find_session_directory, session_id, base_path)

              expect(result).to eq(matching_dir)
            end

            it "returns nil when no matching directory exists" do
              result = session_manager.send(:find_session_directory, "nonexistent", base_path)

              expect(result).to be_nil
            end
          end

          describe "#parse_session_metadata" do
            let(:metadata_file) { File.join(temp_dir, "test.meta") }

            context "when metadata is valid" do
              let(:valid_content) do
                <<~METADATA
                  timestamp: 2024-01-01T12:00:00Z
                  focus: code
                  target: HEAD~1..HEAD
                  context: auto
                METADATA
              end

              before do
                File.write(metadata_file, valid_content)
              end

              it "parses metadata correctly" do
                result = session_manager.send(:parse_session_metadata, metadata_file)

                expect(result).to eq({
                  timestamp: "2024-01-01T12:00:00Z",
                  focus: "code",
                  target: "HEAD~1..HEAD",
                  context: "auto"
                })
              end
            end

            context "when metadata is malformed" do
              let(:invalid_content) { "invalid content without proper format" }

              before do
                File.write(metadata_file, invalid_content)
              end

              it "returns nil for malformed metadata" do
                result = session_manager.send(:parse_session_metadata, metadata_file)

                expect(result).to be_nil
              end
            end

            context "when file does not exist" do
              it "returns nil for missing file" do
                result = session_manager.send(:parse_session_metadata, "/nonexistent/file")

                expect(result).to be_nil
              end
            end

            context "when file cannot be read" do
              before do
                File.write(metadata_file, "content")
                allow(File).to receive(:read).with(metadata_file).and_raise(StandardError, "Read error")
              end

              it "returns nil on read error" do
                result = session_manager.send(:parse_session_metadata, metadata_file)

                expect(result).to be_nil
              end
            end
          end

          describe "#create_session_files" do
            let(:session_dir) { File.join(temp_dir, "test_session") }
            let(:test_session) do
              CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewSession.new(
                session_id: "review-20240101-120000",
                session_name: "code-HEAD~1..HEAD-20240101-120000",
                timestamp: "2024-01-01T12:00:00Z",
                directory_path: session_dir,
                focus: "code",
                target: "HEAD~1..HEAD",
                context_mode: "auto"
              )
            end

            before do
              FileUtils.mkdir_p(session_dir)
            end

            it "creates README file with session information" do
              session_manager.send(:create_session_files, test_session)

              readme_path = File.join(session_dir, "README.md")
              expect(File.exist?(readme_path)).to be true

              content = File.read(readme_path)
              expect(content).to include("# Code Review Session: code-HEAD~1..HEAD-20240101-120000")
              expect(content).to include("**Generated**: 2024-01-01T12:00:00Z")
              expect(content).to include("**Target**: HEAD~1..HEAD")
              expect(content).to include("**Focus**: code")
              expect(content).to include("**Context**: auto")
            end

            it "includes session files documentation" do
              session_manager.send(:create_session_files, test_session)

              readme_path = File.join(session_dir, "README.md")
              content = File.read(readme_path)

              expect(content).to include("## Session Files")
              expect(content).to include("session.meta")
              expect(content).to include("input.diff")
              expect(content).to include("prompt.md")
              expect(content).to include("cr-report-*.md")
            end

            it "includes next steps information" do
              session_manager.send(:create_session_files, test_session)

              readme_path = File.join(session_dir, "README.md")
              content = File.read(readme_path)

              expect(content).to include("## Next Steps")
              expect(content).to include("code-review command")
            end
          end
        end

        describe "integration scenarios" do
          context "complete session lifecycle" do
            let(:focus) { "code tests" }
            let(:target) { "HEAD~2..HEAD" }
            let(:context_mode) { "auto" }

            before do
              # Mock session builder to return a realistic session
              allow(mock_session_builder).to receive(:build_full_session) do |f, t, c, bp|
                session_dir = File.join(bp, "#{f.tr(" ", "-")}-#{t.gsub("~", "tilde").gsub("..", "-to-")}-20240101-120000")
                # Create the directory since create_session_files expects it to exist
                FileUtils.mkdir_p(session_dir)

                CodingAgentTools::Models::Code::ReviewSession.new(
                  session_id: "review-20240101-120000",
                  session_name: "#{f.tr(" ", "-")}-#{t.gsub("~", "tilde").gsub("..", "-to-")}-20240101-120000",
                  timestamp: "2024-01-01T12:00:00Z",
                  directory_path: session_dir,
                  focus: f,
                  target: t,
                  context_mode: c,
                  metadata: {created_at: Time.now}
                )
              end
            end

            it "can create, list, and load sessions" do
              # Create session
              session = session_manager.create_session({
                focus: focus,
                target: target,
                context_mode: context_mode,
                base_path: base_path
              })

              expect(session.focus).to eq(focus)
              expect(session.target).to eq(target)

              # Create the actual directory and metadata for listing test
              FileUtils.mkdir_p(session.directory_path)
              metadata_content = <<~METADATA
                timestamp: #{session.timestamp}
                focus: #{session.focus}
                target: #{session.target}
                context: #{session.context_mode}
              METADATA
              File.write(File.join(session.directory_path, "session.meta"), metadata_content)

              # List sessions should include our created session
              sessions = session_manager.list_sessions(base_path)
              expect(sessions.size).to eq(1)
              expect(sessions.first[:focus]).to eq(focus)

              # Load session should work
              # Need to mock the find_session_directory method to return the correct path
              allow(session_manager).to receive(:find_session_directory).with(session.session_id, base_path).and_return(session.directory_path)

              loaded_session = session_manager.load_session(session.session_id, base_path)
              expect(loaded_session).not_to be_nil
              expect(loaded_session.focus).to eq(focus)
              expect(loaded_session.target).to eq(target)
            end
          end

          context "error handling throughout lifecycle" do
            it "handles session builder errors gracefully" do
              allow(mock_session_builder).to receive(:build_full_session).and_raise(StandardError, "Builder failed")

              expect do
                session_manager.create_session(session_params)
              end.to raise_error(StandardError, "Builder failed")
            end

            it "handles file system errors in listing" do
              # Create a session directory but make it unreadable
              session_dir = File.join(base_path, "unreadable-session-20240101-120000")
              FileUtils.mkdir_p(session_dir)

              # Create metadata file
              metadata_path = File.join(session_dir, "session.meta")
              File.write(metadata_path, "timestamp: 2024-01-01T12:00:00Z\nfocus: code\ntarget: HEAD")

              # Mock File.read to raise an error
              allow(File).to receive(:read).and_call_original
              allow(File).to receive(:read).with(metadata_path).and_raise(StandardError, "Permission denied")

              # Should handle the error gracefully and skip the problematic session
              sessions = session_manager.list_sessions(base_path)
              expect(sessions).to be_empty
            end
          end
        end
      end
  - path: dev-tools/spec/coding_agent_tools/organisms/code_quality/agent_coordination_foundation_spec.rb
    size: 24689
    content: |
      # frozen_string_literal: true

      require "spec_helper"
      require "coding_agent_tools/organisms/code_quality/agent_coordination_foundation"

      RSpec.describe CodingAgentTools::Organisms::CodeQuality::AgentCoordinationFoundation do
        let(:foundation) { described_class.new }

        describe "#initialize" do
          it "initializes empty agent registry" do
            expect(foundation.instance_variable_get(:@agent_registry)).to eq({})
          end

          it "initializes empty error assignments" do
            expect(foundation.instance_variable_get(:@error_assignments)).to eq({})
          end

          it "initializes empty completion status" do
            expect(foundation.instance_variable_get(:@completion_status)).to eq({})
          end

          it "sets default no-op hooks" do
            expect(foundation.on_error_file_ready).to be_a(Proc)
            expect(foundation.on_agent_complete).to be_a(Proc)
            expect(foundation.on_all_agents_complete).to be_a(Proc)
          end

          it "allows hook assignment" do
            hook = ->(_file, _agent_id) { puts "Hook called" }
            foundation.on_error_file_ready = hook
            expect(foundation.on_error_file_ready).to eq(hook)
          end
        end

        describe "#register_agent" do
          it "registers an agent with default values" do
            foundation.register_agent("agent1")

            registry = foundation.instance_variable_get(:@agent_registry)
            expect(registry["agent1"]).to eq({
              id: "agent1",
              capabilities: [],
              status: :idle,
              assigned_file: nil
            })
          end

          it "registers an agent with capabilities" do
            foundation.register_agent("agent2", capabilities: ["ruby", "javascript"])

            registry = foundation.instance_variable_get(:@agent_registry)
            expect(registry["agent2"]).to eq({
              id: "agent2",
              capabilities: ["ruby", "javascript"],
              status: :idle,
              assigned_file: nil
            })
          end

          it "allows registering multiple agents" do
            foundation.register_agent("agent1")
            foundation.register_agent("agent2", capabilities: ["python"])

            registry = foundation.instance_variable_get(:@agent_registry)
            expect(registry.keys).to contain_exactly("agent1", "agent2")
          end

          it "overwrites existing agent registration" do
            foundation.register_agent("agent1", capabilities: ["ruby"])
            foundation.register_agent("agent1", capabilities: ["python"])

            registry = foundation.instance_variable_get(:@agent_registry)
            expect(registry["agent1"][:capabilities]).to eq(["python"])
          end
        end

        describe "#assign_error_files" do
          context "with no agents registered" do
            it "returns error when no agents are available" do
              result = foundation.assign_error_files(["error1.txt", "error2.txt"])

              expect(result).to eq({
                success: false,
                error: "No agents registered"
              })
            end
          end

          context "with registered agents" do
            before do
              foundation.register_agent("agent1")
              foundation.register_agent("agent2")
            end

            it "assigns files to agents in round-robin fashion" do
              files = ["error1.txt", "error2.txt", "error3.txt", "error4.txt"]
              result = foundation.assign_error_files(files)

              expect(result[:success]).to be true
              expect(result[:assignments]["agent1"]).to eq(["error1.txt", "error3.txt"])
              expect(result[:assignments]["agent2"]).to eq(["error2.txt", "error4.txt"])
              expect(result[:agents_used]).to eq(2)
            end

            it "updates agent status to assigned" do
              foundation.assign_error_files(["error1.txt"])

              registry = foundation.instance_variable_get(:@agent_registry)
              expect(registry["agent1"][:status]).to eq(:assigned)
              expect(registry["agent1"][:assigned_file]).to eq("error1.txt")
            end

            it "calls on_error_file_ready hook for each assignment" do
              hook_calls = []
              foundation.on_error_file_ready = ->(file, agent_id) { hook_calls << [file, agent_id] }

              foundation.assign_error_files(["error1.txt", "error2.txt"])

              expect(hook_calls).to contain_exactly(
                ["error1.txt", "agent1"],
                ["error2.txt", "agent2"]
              )
            end

            it "stores error assignments" do
              foundation.assign_error_files(["error1.txt", "error2.txt"])

              assignments = foundation.instance_variable_get(:@error_assignments)
              expect(assignments).to eq({
                "agent1" => ["error1.txt"],
                "agent2" => ["error2.txt"]
              })
            end

            it "handles single file assignment" do
              result = foundation.assign_error_files(["error1.txt"])

              expect(result[:success]).to be true
              expect(result[:assignments]["agent1"]).to eq(["error1.txt"])
              expect(result[:agents_used]).to eq(1)
            end

            it "handles more files than agents" do
              files = ["error1.txt", "error2.txt", "error3.txt"]
              result = foundation.assign_error_files(files)

              expect(result[:success]).to be true
              expect(result[:assignments]["agent1"]).to eq(["error1.txt", "error3.txt"])
              expect(result[:assignments]["agent2"]).to eq(["error2.txt"])
              expect(result[:agents_used]).to eq(2)
            end

            it "handles empty file list" do
              result = foundation.assign_error_files([])

              expect(result[:success]).to be true
              expect(result[:assignments]).to eq({})
              expect(result[:agents_used]).to eq(0)
            end
          end
        end

        describe "#mark_agent_complete" do
          let(:agent_results) { {fixes_applied: 5, errors_resolved: 3} }

          before do
            foundation.register_agent("agent1")
            foundation.register_agent("agent2")
          end

          context "with valid agent" do
            it "updates agent status to complete" do
              foundation.mark_agent_complete("agent1", agent_results)

              registry = foundation.instance_variable_get(:@agent_registry)
              expect(registry["agent1"][:status]).to eq(:complete)
            end

            it "stores completion status with timestamp" do
